# About Click2Call
Click2Call is an Australian business communications company providing AI-enhanced Cloud PBX, VoIP, and SIP Trunking services to businesses of all sizes across Australia. We are operated by Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108).
## Key Takeaways
- Click2Call is an Australian-owned business communications company trading as Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108).
- We provide Cloud PBX, AI Voice Tools, AI Receptionist, SIP Trunks, and Microsoft Teams Calling to businesses across Australia.
- Our mission is to make enterprise-grade communications accessible and affordable for Australian businesses of all sizes.
- All services are available Australia-wide with no lock-in contracts and a 30-day free trial.
- Contact us on 1300 884 879 or support@click2call.com.au for a free consultation.

What Is Click2Call
## What Is Click2Call?
Click2Call is a modern, cloud-based business communications platform built specifically for Australian businesses. We provide everything a business needs to manage its phone communications — from a full-featured Cloud PBX with auto-attendants and call recording, to AI-powered voice tools that transcribe, summarise, and analyse every call.
Unlike large global providers, we are focused entirely on the Australian market. This means our products are designed around the Australian telecommunications landscape, our support team is based in Australia, and our infrastructure is hosted locally for optimal performance and data sovereignty.
Our Mission
## Our Mission
"To give every Australian business access to enterprise-grade communications technology that is simple, reliable, and intelligently powered by AI."
We believe that powerful business communications should not be reserved for large corporations with big IT budgets. Our mission is to democratise enterprise-grade phone systems for Australian SMBs — making them accessible, affordable, and easy to manage. Read our customer case studies to see the difference we make.
What We Offer
## What We Offer
Cloud PBX A fully hosted business phone system with auto-attendant, call recording, voicemail-to-email, and more.
AI Voice Tools Real-time transcription, call summaries, sentiment analysis, and smart routing powered by AI.
AI Receptionist A 24/7 intelligent virtual receptionist that answers, routes, and handles calls using natural language.
SIP Trunks Connect your existing on-premise PBX to our carrier network for cost-effective call termination.
Microsoft Teams Calling Add Australian phone numbers and PSTN calling to Microsoft Teams via Direct Routing.
VoIP Phone Systems Modern VoIP solutions for businesses of all sizes, available Australia-wide.
Who We Serve
## Who We Serve
We serve Australian businesses of all sizes, from sole traders and startups to established SMBs and multi-site organisations. Our services are available in every state and territory, including all major cities — Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, Canberra, Gold Coast, and Hobart — as well as regional and rural areas.
Small Business (1–10 staff)
Medium Business (10–50 staff)
Multi-Site Organisations
Remote & Hybrid Teams
Startups
Professional Services
Why We Built Click2Call
## Why We Built Click2Call
We built Click2Call because we saw too many Australian businesses struggling with outdated, overpriced, and overly complex phone systems. Small and medium businesses were being forced to choose between expensive enterprise solutions with features they did not need, or basic consumer-grade services that lacked the reliability and features their businesses demanded.
We believed there had to be a better way. By combining the reliability of a carrier-grade network with the intelligence of modern AI and the simplicity of a well-designed product, we set out to build the phone system we always wished existed for Australian businesses.

Meet the Team
## Meet the Team
Click2Call is built and run by a small, experienced Australian team with deep roots in telecommunications, business, and technology. Every decision — from the platform architecture to the pricing model — is made by people who have spent years working directly with Australian businesses and understanding what they actually need from a phone system.

Royce oversees the network architecture, carrier relationships, and technical quality of the Click2Call service.

At Click2Call, Ollie drives the company’s growth, shapes the customer experience, and ensures the business is always moving in the right direction. She is the first point of contact for new business enquiries and partnership opportunities.

Programmer

With 5 years of experience in programming, API development, and artificial intelligence, he is responsible for building the tools that make Click2Call smarter — from the AI Voice Tools and AI Receptionist to the web portal and third-party integrations. Daniel’s work ensures the platform stays ahead of the curve as AI continues to reshape business communications.

SECTION 1: HERO
[Image: Futuristic AI reception desk]
Answers calls 24/7 · No staff needed
# AI Receptionist for Australian Businesses
Click2Call's AI Receptionist is a conversational AI that answers every call to your business with a natural, professional voice, 24/7. It intelligently handles caller enquiries, from routing calls and answering common questions to taking detailed messages after hours, ensuring you never miss an opportunity.
Get Started
View Pricing
SECTION 2: KEY TAKEAWAYS
Key Takeaways
- 24/7 Call Answering: The AI Receptionist answers every call instantly, day or night, ensuring no lead is ever missed and every customer is greeted professionally.
- Conversational AI: Unlike a rigid IVR menu, the AI Receptionist understands natural language, allowing callers to state their needs in their own words.
- Intelligent Routing: It can answer frequently asked questions, take detailed messages, or route the caller to the correct person, department, or even a specific mobile number.
- Included Free with Every Phone Line: The AI Receptionist is included at no extra charge with every Click2Call Cloud PBX phone line. Standalone AI receptionist tools from other providers typically cost $100–$300 per month on top of your phone bill.
- Fully Customisable: Business owners can easily configure the AI's scripts, responses, and call-handling logic through the Click2Call web portal.

SECTION 3: WHAT IS AN AI RECEPTIONIST?
## What Is an AI Receptionist?
An AI receptionist is a sophisticated evolution of traditional call handling systems. It is a conversational AI voice agent designed to be the first point of contact for your business, answering phone calls with a natural, professional voice around the clock. Its purpose is to manage inbound calls efficiently and intelligently, freeing up your team to focus on their core work rather than answering the phone.
You may also hear the term AI voice agent used to describe this technology. The two terms refer to the same thing: an AI that communicates by voice, understands natural speech, and takes action based on what a caller says. When an AI voice agent is configured specifically to handle business calls — greeting callers, answering questions, routing calls, and taking messages — it is commonly called an AI receptionist.
How is it different from a traditional IVR or Auto-Attendant?
A traditional IVR (Interactive Voice Response) or auto-attendant forces callers into a rigid, numeric menu: "Press 1 for Sales, Press 2 for Support." This can be frustrating and inefficient, particularly for callers who are not sure which option applies to them. An AI receptionist, by contrast, uses Natural Language Understanding (NLU). A caller can simply say, "I've got a question about my last invoice," and the AI understands their intent. It can then provide a direct answer, or route the call to the accounts team, without the caller ever needing to press a button.
This conversational ability makes the experience feel more personal and efficient. The AI can handle complex queries, book appointments, answer FAQs, and only escalate to a human when a query requires a level of nuance or authority it does not have. It is the difference between a smart, helpful assistant and a simple call-directing machine.
Built into your phone system — not bolted on.
Most standalone AI receptionist tools require you to forward your calls to a third-party service, which then handles the call before passing it back to your phone system. Click2Call's AI Receptionist is different. It is built directly into your Click2Call Cloud PBX phone system. This means the AI and your phone system share the same call flows, extensions, ring groups, and voicemail. There is no forwarding, no latency, and no disconnected experience. When the AI transfers a call to a member of your team, it transfers it internally — just as a human receptionist sitting at a desk would.
All call data is processed on Australian infrastructure and remains subject to Australian privacy law. Your business information, caller data, and call recordings are never shared with overseas AI platforms.
SECTION 4: WHAT CAN IT DO?
## What Can an AI Receptionist Do?
A powerful first impression that works around the clock.
### 24/7 Call Answering
Instantly answers every call, ensuring no customer ever reaches voicemail during or after hours.
### Intelligent Call Routing
Understands who the caller wants to speak to and transfers them to the right person or department.
### Appointment Booking
Integrates with your calendar to book, reschedule, or cancel appointments based on your availability.
### Answering FAQs
Provides instant answers to common questions about your business hours, location, or services.
### Taking Messages
Takes detailed messages from callers and sends them instantly to your email or SMS with a transcript.
### Lead Qualification
Asks callers a series of qualifying questions and routes high-value leads directly to your sales team.
SECTION 5: COMPARISON TABLE
## AI Receptionist vs Human Receptionist
| Feature | Human Receptionist | Click2Call AI Receptionist |
|---||---||---|
| Cost | $55k+ per year + super + leave | From $10/month (ex GST) |
| Availability | 8 hours/day, 5 days/week | 24 hours/day, 7 days/week |
| Consistency | Variable, depends on mood/workload | 100% consistent every time |
| Scalability | Can only handle one call at a time | Can handle hundreds of calls simultaneously |
| Training Needed | Weeks to months | Ready in minutes |
| Response Time | Multiple rings, may go to voicemail | Answers instantly on the first ring |
| After-hours Coverage | None (goes to voicemail) | Full service (takes messages, answers FAQs) |

SECTION 6: USE CASES
## Perfect for Any Service-Based Business
### Trades & Construction
Capture every job lead, even when you're on-site. The AI can qualify the caller and book a time for a quote.
### Medical & Health Clinics
Handle appointment bookings and answer common patient questions about opening hours or location, freeing up admin staff.
### Legal & Accounting Firms
Provide a professional front door and route new client enquiries to the right solicitor or accountant automatically.
### Real Estate Agencies
Answer tenant and buyer enquiries 24/7, book property viewings, and take maintenance requests after hours.
### E-commerce & Retail
Handle common customer service queries like "Where is my order?" without tying up your support team.
### Hospitality (Restaurants & Hotels)
Take bookings, provide information on opening times, and answer questions about your menu or room availability.
SECTION 7: HOW IT WORKS
## How Does Click2Call's AI Receptionist Work?
### Configure Your AI
Use our simple web portal to define your business hours, FAQs, and how you want calls handled.
### Divert Your Number
Forward your existing business number to the unique number we provide for your AI Receptionist.
### AI Answers Calls
The AI answers instantly, greets the caller, and understands their request in natural language.
### Action is Taken
The call is routed, a message is taken, or an answer is provided, exactly as you configured.
SECTION 8: FAQ
## AI Receptionist FAQs
FAQ Item
What is an AI receptionist?
An AI Receptionist is a conversational AI that answers your business phone calls, interacts with callers in natural language, and performs tasks like answering common questions, taking messages, booking appointments, and routing calls to the right person or department.
FAQ Item
What is the difference between an AI receptionist and an AI voice agent?
The terms are often used interchangeably. An AI voice agent is the underlying technology — a conversational AI that communicates by voice. An AI receptionist is the business application of that technology: an AI voice agent configured specifically to answer business calls, greet callers, route enquiries, and handle common requests. Click2Call's AI Receptionist is powered by an AI voice agent.
FAQ Item
How is an AI receptionist different from an auto-attendant or IVR?
An IVR or auto-attendant uses a rigid, button-based menu (e.g., 'Press 1 for Sales'). An AI Receptionist is conversational; callers can simply state what they need in their own words (e.g., 'I'd like to speak to someone about my bill') and the AI understands the intent and routes the call accordingly.
FAQ Item
Can an AI receptionist handle Australian accents?
Yes, our AI models are specifically trained on a wide variety of Australian accents and dialects to ensure high comprehension accuracy.
FAQ Item
Does the AI receptionist work after hours and on weekends?
Yes, the AI Receptionist operates 24/7/365, ensuring every call to your business is answered professionally, even outside of standard business hours.
FAQ Item
Can callers tell they are speaking to an AI?
Our AI uses advanced text-to-speech technology to sound natural and conversational. While it is an AI, the experience is designed to be smooth and professional. We recommend including a brief disclosure that the caller is speaking with a virtual assistant.
FAQ Item
How do I set up an AI receptionist with Click2Call?
Setup is simple and done through our web portal. You define the call flows, write the scripts for your AI, and specify how different types of calls should be handled. Our support team is also available to help you get started.
FAQ Item
Can the AI receptionist transfer calls to a real person?
Absolutely. The AI can transfer calls to any individual, department, ring group, or external number based on the caller's request or pre-defined rules. If a caller asks to speak to a human, it can escalate the call immediately.
FAQ Item
How much does an AI receptionist cost compared to hiring a receptionist?
Most standalone AI receptionist services charge between $100 and $300 per month, on top of your existing phone bill. Click2Call's AI Receptionist is different — it is included at no extra charge with every Cloud PBX phone line. You are not paying a separate subscription for the AI; it comes with the phone line. Combined with the elimination of salary, superannuation, leave, and training costs compared to a human receptionist, the savings are substantial. See our pricing page for full details.
FAQ Item
Is an AI receptionist suitable for a small business?
Yes, it's ideal for small businesses. It provides the professional front-end of a large company without the high cost, ensuring you never miss a lead and can focus on your core work instead of answering the phone.
FAQ Item
Can I customise what the AI receptionist says?
Yes, you have full control over the scripts, greetings, and answers the AI provides. You can configure it through the Click2Call portal to match your brand voice and business logic.
FAQ Item
What happens if the AI can't answer a caller's question?
If the AI encounters a question it hasn't been trained on, it can be configured to offer a transfer to a human agent, take a detailed message for a callback, or provide alternative contact information.
FAQ Item
Is an AI receptionist available on a month-to-month basis?
Yes. Like all Click2Call services, the AI Receptionist fee is deducted from your prepaid account credit each month. There are no lock-in contracts and no fixed terms — you can cancel at any time.
Have more questions? View all FAQs for answers to common questions about our services.
SECTION: RELATED AI TOOLS

=================== HERO ===================
[Image: AI call analytics dashboard on a laptop screen]

# Know Exactly What Happened on Every Call
Stop re-listening to recordings. Click2Call automatically transcribes every call, writes you a plain-English summary, and shows you how the conversation went — all inside your portal. Free with every account.
Get Started Free
View Pricing
=================== MAIN CONTENT ===================
KEY TAKEAWAYS
Key Takeaways
- Free on every account: After each call you get a written transcript, a 2-sentence summary, and a score showing how the conversation went — automatically, no setup required.
- Want more accuracy? Upgrade to Premium ($15/month) to get the most accurate transcription, see exactly who said what on each call, and transcribe your past recordings.
- Sound professional instantly: Type your hold message or voicemail greeting, choose an AI voice to read it, and it's ready in seconds — no recording studio needed.
- Never miss a call: The AI Receptionist answers your phone, listens to what the caller needs, and transfers them to the right person — even when you're busy or after hours.
- Nothing to install: It's all built into your existing Click2Call account. No new hardware, no IT setup, no extra cost.

WHAT ARE AI VOICE TOOLS
## Your Calls, Automatically Documented
Every time you finish a call, Click2Call's AI goes to work. It reads the recording, writes out everything that was said, and gives you a quick summary so you know exactly what happened — without having to listen back.
Each call gets a written transcript you can read and search, a mood score out of 10 so you can see if the call went well, a topic tag like "Callback Request" or "Customer Complaint" added automatically, and a 2-sentence summary so you can catch up in seconds instead of minutes.
There's more too. Set up an AI Receptionist to answer your phone and transfer callers automatically. Use AI Speech to create hold messages and greetings by just typing what you want said. And set up call alerts so you get an email if a call goes badly or a specific word gets mentioned.
SEE IT IN ACTION — RECORDINGS SCREENSHOT
## See It In Action
This is a real call record from the Click2Call portal. Every inbound and outbound call is automatically analysed and presented like this.
Left: annotated screenshot
[Image: Click2Call portal showing a real call record with AI-generated summary, sentiment score, speaker names, and category tags]
Right: callouts
AI-Generated Summary
After every call you get a short plain-English summary — read it in 10 seconds instead of listening to the whole thing.
How Did the Call Go? (1–10)
The AI scores every call from 1 to 10 based on how it went. Low scores get your attention. High scores show what's working.
Speaker Identification
See who said what, and which names or businesses came up during the conversation.
Automatic Category Tagging
Calls are tagged by topic — such as Business Operations, Customer Support, or Partnership Inquiry — without any manual input.
AI CALL REPORTS SCREENSHOT
## See How Your Team Is Performing
The reports dashboard shows you exactly how your calls are going — for your whole team or per person. See who's having the best conversations, what customers are calling about most, and how long calls are running.
If one person's scores are consistently low, that's a conversation worth having. If the same topic keeps coming up in calls, that's something to look into. Export everything to a spreadsheet when you need it.
- Who's having the best conversations — by person
- What your customers are calling about most
- How long each person's calls are running
- Which names and businesses came up in calls
- Export to a spreadsheet anytime

[Image: Click2Call AI Call Reports dashboard showing sentiment scores by user and call volume by category]
AI SPEECH SCREENSHOT
[Image: Click2Call AI Speech tool showing voice actor selection with options including Alice, Emma, Isabella, Lily, Annmarie, Gracie, and Sofia]
## Create a Professional Phone Voice in Seconds
Type what you want your phone to say — your hold message, voicemail greeting, or welcome announcement — choose a voice, and click Generate. Done. No recording booth, no voice talent, no waiting.
Choose from voices including Alice, Emma, Isabella, Lily, Annmarie, Gracie, Sofia, Asya and more — male and female options available. Use commas and full stops in your text to add natural pauses.
Perfect if you're setting up a new phone system and want it to sound professional from day one — or if you update your messages regularly and don't want to re-record every time.
AI RECEPTIONIST SECTION (text only, no screenshot)
## Answer Every Call, Even When You Can't
The AI Receptionist picks up your phone, asks the caller what they need, and transfers them to the right person — automatically. It works 24/7, so customers always get answered even when you're busy, with a client, or after hours.
Setup takes about 10 minutes. Write a greeting, pick a voice, and describe your departments — like "Sales", "Support", or "Accounts". When someone calls and says what they need, the AI knows where to send them.
Always On
Runs 24/7 by default. Set it to business hours only if you prefer.
Your Choice of Voice
Pick from a range of male and female voices so it sounds like your brand.
Callers Get to the Right Person
No pressing 1 for sales, 2 for support. The caller says what they need and the AI figures it out.
Allow Transfers to User Extensions
Optionally allow the AI to transfer callers directly to individual staff extensions when identified.
AI VOICEMAIL + ALERTS — two-column cards
AI Voicemail
### AI Voicemail
AI Voicemail gives every person on your team a personalised greeting — without anyone having to record anything. Type the message once and it automatically uses each person's name for their extension. Set separate messages for when a line is busy or unavailable.
Voicemails are also transcribed automatically so you can read them instead of listening back — and search across all messages by keyword.
Transcription Alerts
### Transcription Alerts
Get an email alert the moment a call goes wrong. Set it up to notify you when a specific word or phrase is mentioned, when a call scores below a certain level, or when the AI detects a problem. You set the rules — the AI watches every call so you don't have to.
Great for trades businesses, real estate offices, and anyone who can't listen back to every call but still wants to know when something important happens.

## Frequently Asked Questions
What AI voice tools does Click2Call offer?
Click2Call offers a full suite of AI tools built into the portal: call transcription, AI-generated call summaries, sentiment analysis, profanity analysis, auto language translation, voicemail transcription, AI Speech (text-to-voice with multiple voice actors), AI Receptionist (automated call answering and routing), AI Agents, AI Chat, AI Voicemail, and Transcription Alerts. A Free tier is included with every Cloud PBX plan, and a Premium tier unlocks additional capabilities.
What is the difference between the Free and Premium AI tiers?
The Free tier includes call transcriptions, sentiment analysis, transcription summaries, profanity analysis, auto language translation, and voicemail transcriptions — all at no extra cost. The Premium tier adds speaker diarisation (identifying individual speakers by name), the most accurate transcription model, priority processing, and the ability to transcribe historical recordings retroactively.
What is the AI Receptionist?
The AI Receptionist handles incoming calls by greeting the caller, understanding their request using voice recognition, and routing them to the appropriate department or extension. You configure a welcome message, choose an AI voice actor, set timeout and retry behaviour, and define which lines connect directly to the receptionist. It operates at all times and requires no human involvement.
What is AI Speech?
AI Speech lets you generate professional voice recordings directly from text. You type your message, choose a gender and voice actor (options include Alice, Emma, Isabella, Lily, Annmarie, Gracie, Sofia, and more), and click Generate AI Voice. The recording can then be used as a hold message, voicemail greeting, or IVR prompt — no recording studio required.
How does AI call transcription work?
When a call is recorded, the audio is automatically processed by Click2Call's AI engine. It converts speech to text, identifies speakers, applies punctuation, and generates a searchable transcript. The system also produces an AI summary, tags the call with a category (such as Callback Request or Account Management), and scores the sentiment on a scale of 1 to 10. All of this happens automatically after the call ends.
Do I need extra hardware for AI voice tools?
No. All AI voice tools are cloud-based software features built into the Click2Call portal. They work with your existing Cloud PBX service and require no additional phones, servers, or on-premise hardware.
Is call recording and transcription legal in Australia?
Call recording laws vary by state in Australia. Generally, you must notify all parties that the call is being recorded. Click2Call allows you to play an automatic notification message at the start of each call to help with compliance. We strongly recommend seeking independent legal advice to ensure you meet all your obligations.
Does Click2Call offer a free trial for AI Voice Tools?
Yes. Click2Call accounts are free to create with no credit card required. The Free AI tier is included automatically with every account. You can sign up at portal.click2call.com.au/join and start using the AI tools immediately.
Can I search my call transcripts?
Yes. All transcripts are fully searchable from the Click2Call portal. Search by keyword, date, user, or call category to find specific conversations without listening to recordings.
What are Transcription Alerts?
Transcription Alerts send you an email when the AI detects something specific in a call — like a keyword being mentioned, a call scoring below a level you set, or a problem being flagged. You choose the rules and who gets notified.
How secure is my call data?
Click2Call's AI runs on Australian-hosted infrastructure. Your call recordings and transcripts are only accessible to authorised users in your account. We don't sell or share your call data with anyone.
Is call recording legal in Australia?
Laws vary by state but generally you need to notify all parties a call is being recorded. Click2Call lets you set an automatic message at the start of each call to cover this. We recommend getting legal advice to confirm your obligations in your state.
Have more questions? Contact our support team for help with setup and configuration.
## Try It Free — No Credit Card Needed
Sign up, make a call, and see your first transcript and summary appear in your portal. Takes about 5 minutes to get started.
Get Started Free
[Image: AI call analytics dashboard showing transcription, sentiment score and speaker identification]

=================== END FOOTER ===================

## Choosing a 1300 Number Provider in Australia | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/1300-number-providers
**Description:** A guide for Australian businesses on what to look for when choosing a 1300 number provider, from call rates and features to customer support.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
Business VoIP
# Choosing a 1300 Number Provider in Australia
A guide for Australian businesses on what to look for when choosing a 1300 number provider, from call rates and features to customer support.
By Royce Clark
Published on 05 Feb 2026
Article Body
A 1300 number gives your business a professional, national presence and makes it easier for customers to contact you. But with so many providers in Australia, how do you choose the right one? This guide breaks down the key factors to consider.
## 1. Understand the Costs
Pricing for 1300 numbers typically has a few components:
- Monthly Rental Fee: A fixed charge to keep the number active. This can range from $10 to $30 per month.
- Inbound Call Rates: What you pay to receive calls. These are often charged per minute and can vary depending on whether the call originates from a landline or a mobile.
- Setup Fee: A one-time charge to activate the number.

Look for providers with transparent, competitive pricing. Some providers, like Click2Call, bundle a 1300 number and include a generous number of inbound minutes with their Cloud PBX plans, which can be much more cost-effective than paying for them separately.
## 2. Check the Included Features
A 1300 number is more than just a number; it's the front door to your business phone system. Your provider should offer a robust set of features to manage your calls effectively:
- Call Routing: The ability to direct calls based on time of day, caller location (geo-routing), or menu selections (IVR).
- Voicemail to Email: Receive voicemail messages as audio files directly in your email inbox.
- Reporting and Analytics: Access to data on call volumes, peak times, and missed calls to help you manage staffing and performance.
- Integration with a Phone System: The best value comes from a provider that bundles your 1300 number with a Cloud PBX system. This gives you a complete, unified solution with features like call transfer, conferencing, and softphone apps.

## 3. Evaluate the Provider's Network and Support
Call quality and reliability are paramount. Choose a provider that operates a high-quality, carrier-grade network within Australia. Equally important is the level of customer support they offer.
- Australian-Based Support: When you need help, you want to speak to someone who understands the local context and can resolve your issue quickly.
- Support Channels: Do they offer phone, email, and ticket-based support? What are their standard support hours?
- Self-Service Portal: A good provider will give you an online portal to manage your number, change routing settings, and view call reports yourself.

## 4. Can You Port Your Existing Number?
If you already have a 1300 number, you'll want to bring it with you. Ensure the provider offers number porting and has a clear, managed process for transferring your number without downtime. The provider should handle the entire process on your behalf.
## Conclusion: Look for Value, Not Just Price
While cost is an important factor, the cheapest provider isn't always the best. Look for a provider that offers a combination of competitive pricing, a strong feature set, and excellent local support. Bundling your 1300 number with a Cloud PBX system from a single provider like Click2Call often provides the best value, simplifying your billing and giving you a seamless, powerful communication platform for your entire business.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
What's the difference between a 1300 and 1800 number?
With a 1300 number, the caller pays the cost of a local call, and the business owner pays the rest. With a 1800 number, the call is completely free for the person calling, and the business owner covers the full cost.
Can I get a "smart number" that spells a word?
Yes. These are called phonewords (e.g., 1300-FLOWERS). They are highly memorable but come at a premium price. You can purchase them from government auctions or through specialised providers. Once you own the rights to the number, you can connect it to any provider.
Author Bio
[Image: Royce Clark]
Written by
Royce Clark
Royce Clark has over 15 years of experience working in the telecommunications industry, specialising in VoIP systems. He is a Voice Engineer at Click2Call, helping Australian businesses design and deploy modern, reliable cloud phone systems.

## How AI Is Changing Business Phone Systems | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/ai-in-business-phones
**Description:** Learn how AI is transforming business phone systems. Discover AI-powered features like intelligent call routing, voice analytics, and automated receptionists.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
# How AI Is Changing Business Phone Systems
From automated receptionists to real-time call transcription and sentiment analysis, AI is making business communication smarter, more efficient, and more insightful.
By Royce Clark
Published on 12 Feb 2026
Article Body
Artificial intelligence is no longer science fiction; it's a practical tool that is fundamentally transforming how businesses operate. One of the most significant areas of impact is in business communications. Modern Cloud PBX systems are now integrating powerful AI features that go far beyond simple call routing, delivering unprecedented levels of efficiency, insight, and customer service.
## 1. The 24/7 AI Receptionist
Missed calls are missed opportunities. But hiring a full-time receptionist is a significant expense, especially for small businesses. An AI Receptionist solves this problem by providing a smart, automated front line for your business, 24/7.
Unlike a basic auto-attendant ("Press 1 for..."), an AI Receptionist uses natural language processing to understand a caller's intent. It can answer common questions, route calls to the right person or department, take detailed messages, and even book appointments, all without human intervention. This frees up your team to focus on high-value work while ensuring every customer call is answered professionally.
## 2. Real-Time Call Transcription
Taking notes during an important call can be distracting and lead to missed details. AI-powered real-time transcription automatically converts spoken words into text, creating a searchable record of every conversation. This has several powerful applications:
- Improved Accuracy: Never miss a detail or action item again. You have a complete, word-for-word record of the conversation.
- Compliance and Training: For industries with strict compliance requirements, having a transcript of every call is invaluable. It's also a powerful tool for training new staff, allowing them to review real customer interactions.
- Searchable Archives: Quickly find key information from past calls without having to listen to hours of recordings.

## 3. Sentiment Analysis: Understanding the "How"
A transcript tells you *what* was said, but sentiment analysis tells you *how* it was said. By analysing the tone, pitch, and language used, AI can determine the emotional sentiment of a call—whether the customer is happy, frustrated, or neutral. This provides managers with a powerful tool for quality assurance.
Instead of randomly sampling calls, managers can be automatically alerted to conversations with negative sentiment, allowing them to proactively address customer issues and identify coaching opportunities for their team.
## 4. Action Item and Summary Generation
The next frontier of AI in communications is automated summaries. After a call ends, the AI can analyse the transcript and automatically generate a concise summary of the conversation, including key topics discussed and a list of action items. This summary can be automatically pushed into your CRM or project management software, saving your team hours of manual data entry and follow-up work.
## The Click2Call Advantage
At Click2Call, we believe AI should be a standard feature, not an expensive add-on. Our AI Voice Tools , including real-time transcription and sentiment analysis, are built into our Cloud PBX platform. Our AI Receptionist provides a powerful, customisable solution for managing your inbound calls.
By integrating these tools directly into your phone system, we make it easy for Australian businesses to leverage the power of AI to improve productivity, gain deeper customer insights, and deliver a superior customer experience.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
Is the AI transcription accurate?
Our AI transcription models are highly accurate, especially for Australian accents. While no transcription is 100% perfect, it provides a highly reliable and searchable record of your conversations.
Is my call data secure?
Yes. All call data, including recordings and transcripts, is encrypted and stored securely in our Australian data centres, complying with local data privacy laws.

## Business Phone System Cost Australia 2026 | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/business-phone-system-cost
**Description:** A detailed breakdown of the costs involved in a business phone system in Australia, including hardware, licensing, call rates, and the total cost of ownership.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
Pricing
# How Much Does a Business Phone System Cost in Australia? (2026 Guide)
A detailed breakdown of the costs involved in a business phone system in Australia, including hardware, licensing, call rates, and the total cost of ownership.
By Royce Clark
Published on 22 Feb 2026
Article Body
Budgeting for a new business phone system can be confusing. With so many options available, from traditional on-premise hardware to modern cloud-based solutions, understanding the true cost is essential. This guide provides a transparent breakdown of what Australian businesses can expect to pay for a phone system in 2026, helping you make an informed decision and avoid hidden fees.
## The Old Way: Traditional PBX Costs
An on-premise PBX system involves a significant upfront investment. While these systems are becoming less common, it's important to understand their cost structure as a baseline.
- Hardware: The physical PBX unit itself can cost anywhere from $5,000 to over $20,000 , depending on the size of your business.
- Handsets: Desk phones can range from $100 for a basic model to over $500 for an executive video phone. For a 10-person office, this adds $1,000 - $5,000.
- Installation: Professional installation and configuration can cost between $1,000 and $5,000 .
- Line Rental: You'll pay ongoing monthly fees for your physical phone lines (ISDN or PSTN), which can be several hundred dollars per month.
- Maintenance: A support contract can cost 15-20% of the initial hardware cost, per year.

The total upfront cost for a 10-person business can easily exceed $10,000 , with significant ongoing fees.
## The New Way: Cloud PBX Pricing
Cloud-based phone systems, like Click2Call, operate on a much simpler and more transparent subscription model. This shifts the cost from a large capital expenditure (CapEx) to a predictable operating expenditure (OpEx).
The primary cost is a per-user, per-month license fee . This fee typically includes everything you need to run your phone system.
### What's Included in a Cloud PBX Plan?
With a provider like Click2Call, your monthly fee covers:
- License to use the platform: Access to all the features of your chosen plan.
- Unlimited Calls: Most plans include unlimited standard calls to Australian local, national, and mobile numbers (subject to a fair use policy).
- Free Software: Use of the mobile app (iOS/Android) and desktop softphone app.
- Support & Maintenance: All support, software updates, and system maintenance are handled by the provider.
- Number Porting: The one-time cost of moving your existing phone number to the new service is usually included.

### Click2Call Pricing in Australia (2026)
Click2Call uses simple, transparent pay-as-you-go pricing — no bundled plans, no lock-in contracts. You pay only for the numbers and channels you use, plus per-minute call rates. All prices ex GST.
| Item | Price (ex GST) |
|---||---|
| Cloud PBX Landline | $10.00 / month |
| Direct Dial Number (DDI) | $5.00 / month |
| PBX Extension | Free |
| Additional Channel | $3.00 / month (2 included free) |
| AUS / NZ Fixed Calls | $0.10 / min |
| AUS / NZ Mobile Calls | $0.20 / min |

All prices ex GST. No lock-in contracts. View full pricing & call rates →
### Other Potential Costs
- Handsets: While not required (you can use the free apps), if you want physical desk phones, you'll need to purchase VoIP-compatible models. These typically cost $150 - $400 per phone .
- Call Rates: Calls to international numbers, 13/1300 numbers, and other premium services are charged on a per-minute basis. These rates are generally much lower than traditional carriers, starting from $0.10/min (ex GST) for Australian and NZ fixed calls.
- Direct Numbers (DDIs): A dedicated Direct Dial Number (DDI) costs $5.00 per month (ex GST) with Click2Call.
- Toll-Free Numbers: 1800 Toll-Free numbers are available — contact Click2Call for current pricing.

## Conclusion: A Clearer Picture
For a 10-person business, moving to a Cloud PBX like Click2Call is significantly more cost-effective than a traditional system. With simple pay-as-you-go pricing — Cloud PBX Landlines from $10.00/month (ex GST) and calls from $0.10/min — there are no large upfront hardware costs, no bundled plans, and no lock-in contracts. You pay only for what you use.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
Are there any hidden fees I should be aware of?
No. With Click2Call, our pricing is transparent. The monthly per-user fee covers all standard features and calls. The only extra costs are for optional hardware, international/premium calls, or additional numbers like DDIs and Toll-Free services.
Is it cheaper to pay annually?
Many providers, including Click2Call, offer a discount for customers who choose to pay for their subscription annually instead of monthly. This can typically save you between 10-15% on your total license fees.

## Cloud PBX vs Traditional Phone System | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/cloud-pbx-vs-traditional
**Description:** Discover the key differences between a modern Cloud PBX and an outdated traditional phone system, from cost and features to scalability and reliability.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
Cloud PBX
# Cloud PBX vs Traditional Phone System: What Australian Businesses Need to Know
Discover the key differences between a modern Cloud PBX and an outdated traditional phone system, from cost and features to scalability and reliability.
By Royce Clark
Published on 23 Feb 2026
Article Body
If your Australian business is still relying on a traditional phone system, you might be missing out on significant cost savings, powerful features, and the flexibility needed to thrive in today's work environment. The world of business communications has shifted decisively to the cloud. Understanding the difference between a modern Cloud PBX and an old-school on-premise system is the first step towards future-proofing your business. This article breaks down everything you need to know.
## What is a Traditional PBX?
A traditional PBX (Private Branch Exchange) is a physical piece of hardware that lives in your office—usually in a server room or communications closet. It connects to the public telephone network via physical lines like ISDN or PSTN. All your office desk phones plug directly into this box. For decades, this was the only way for a business to manage multiple phone lines, extensions, and basic features like call transfers.
However, these systems are a product of a bygone era. They are expensive to purchase and install, difficult to scale, and require specialist technicians for even minor changes. With the NBN rollout effectively making old copper lines obsolete, the writing is on the wall for traditional PBX systems in Australia.
## What is a Cloud PBX?
A Cloud PBX, also known as a Hosted PBX or Virtual PBX, performs all the same functions as a traditional system, but the "brains" of the operation are hosted in a secure, off-site data centre and managed by your provider. Your business connects to this system over your internet connection using VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol) technology.
This model eliminates the need for any bulky, expensive hardware in your office. It transforms your phone system from a capital expense into a simple, predictable monthly operating expense. More importantly, it unlocks a world of advanced features and flexibility that traditional systems simply cannot match.
## Head-to-Head Comparison
Let's compare the two systems across the factors that matter most to Australian businesses.
| Feature | Traditional PBX | Cloud PBX |
|---||---||---|
| Cost | High upfront hardware cost ($5,000 - $20,000+), plus ongoing maintenance and line rental fees. | Low to zero upfront cost. Simple, per-user monthly subscription fee. |
| Features | Basic features like call transfer and voicemail. Advanced features are expensive add-ons. | Rich features included as standard: auto-attendant, call recording, mobile app, video conferencing, and advanced AI tools. |
| Scalability | Difficult and expensive. Adding more users may require new hardware cards or a whole new system. | Highly scalable. Add or remove users instantly through an online portal. |
| Remote Work | Not supported. Tied to the physical office location. | Ideal for remote and hybrid teams. Use your business number on a mobile app or softphone from anywhere with internet. |
| Maintenance | Requires specialist technicians for maintenance, updates, and changes. Can be costly and slow. | Managed entirely by the provider. Updates are automatic and included in the subscription. |

## Why Cloud PBX is the Clear Winner
For almost every Australian business, a Cloud PBX is the superior choice. The total cost of ownership is significantly lower, and the included features provide a level of professionalism and efficiency that was once only available to the largest enterprises. The ability to support remote and hybrid work is no longer a luxury but a necessity, and a Cloud PBX is built for this modern reality.
By moving your communications to the cloud, you are not just upgrading your phone system; you are investing in a more agile, resilient, and future-proof foundation for your business.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
Can I keep my existing business phone number?
Yes, absolutely. We can port your existing local, 1300, or 1800 numbers to our Cloud PBX service with zero downtime. We manage the entire process for you.
What happens if our office internet goes down?
A key benefit of Cloud PBX is call continuity. If your office internet fails, our system can automatically forward all incoming calls to designated mobile numbers or another location, ensuring you never miss a call.

## Why ElevenLabs is the Undisputed Leader in Voice AI | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/elevenlabs-voice-ai-sip-trunk
**Description:** Why ElevenLabs leads the voice AI market, how enterprises use it, and how Click2Call connects it to your phone system via SIP trunk.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
# Why ElevenLabs is the Undisputed Leader in Voice AI
A deep dive into why ElevenLabs dominates the voice AI market, how enterprises use it, and how Click2Call connects it seamlessly to your phone system via SIP trunk.
By Royce Clark
Published on 12 Feb 2026
Article Body
Hero image display
[Image: ElevenLabs Voice AI connecting to a business phone system via SIP trunk]
In the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, one company has emerged as the undisputed leader in voice synthesis: ElevenLabs . From powering conversational AI agents in Fortune 500 companies to giving a voice back to those who have lost it, their technology has set a new standard for realism, speed, and scalability. But what makes them the go-to choice for enterprises, and how can your business leverage this power through your existing phone system? This article explores the meteoric rise of ElevenLabs, why it dominates the market, and how Click2Call seamlessly integrates its capabilities using SIP trunking.
Section divider
---
## The Genesis of a Voice AI Powerhouse
Founded in 2022 by Piotr Dabkowski, a former Google machine learning engineer, and Mati Staniszewski, a former Palantir deployment strategist, ElevenLabs was born from a shared frustration with the poorly dubbed American movies of their Polish childhoods. Their mission was ambitious yet simple: to break down language barriers with technology that could generate high-quality, emotionally resonant spoken audio in any voice and any language. This wasn't just about creating another text-to-speech (TTS) tool; it was about capturing the nuance, intonation, and humanity of speech.
The company quickly gained traction, achieving unicorn status with a valuation exceeding $1 billion within its first two years. By early 2026, after a staggering $500 million Series D funding round led by Sequoia Capital, its valuation had skyrocketed to an estimated $11 billion [1]. This rapid growth wasn't just financial; it was a testament to the tangible superiority of their product.
Pull quote
"While it took Twilio eight years to reach $330M ARR, ElevenLabs achieved this milestone in just over two years."
Source: SaaStr [2]
---
## Why ElevenLabs is the Industry Leader: A Technical Deep Dive
The dominance of ElevenLabs isn't accidental. It's the result of relentless innovation across several key areas that, when combined, create a product that is simply unmatched in the market.
### 1. Unparalleled Voice Quality and Realism
The core differentiator for ElevenLabs is the sheer quality of its generated voices. While competitors often produce robotic or flat-sounding audio, ElevenLabs' models generate speech that is rich in emotional nuance and prosody. It captures the subtle inflections, pauses, and tones that make a voice sound genuinely human. This realism is crucial for applications where user engagement and trust are paramount, such as conversational AI agents, audiobooks, and character voice-overs in gaming.
### 2. Ultra-Low Latency for Real-Time Conversations
For a conversational AI agent to be effective, it must respond instantly. Any perceptible delay shatters the illusion of a natural conversation. This is where ElevenLabs has made significant breakthroughs. Their "Turbo" engine can generate audio with a latency of around 300 milliseconds, while their newer "Flash" TTS engine pushes this even lower [3]. This sub-second response time is critical for creating fluid, real-time interactions, making it possible for AI agents to handle complex customer service calls without frustrating delays.
### 3. Superior Voice Cloning and Customisation
One of the most powerful features of the ElevenLabs platform is its ability to create a digital replica of a specific voice from just a few minutes of audio. This "Voice Cloning" technology allows businesses to create a unique and consistent brand voice for all their audio content. Furthermore, their "Voice Design" tools enable the creation of entirely new, synthetic voices by specifying parameters like gender, age, and accent. This level of customisation is invaluable for companies looking to establish a distinct sonic identity.
### 4. Expansive Language and Accent Support
From its inception, ElevenLabs has focused on breaking down language barriers. The platform supports over 32 languages, allowing businesses to serve a global audience with a single solution. Crucially, the quality remains high across all languages, preserving the emotional depth and clarity of the original voice. This capability is a game-changer for multinational corporations aiming to deliver consistent customer experiences across different regions.
---
### Enterprise Adoption: A Stamp of Approval
The most telling indicator of ElevenLabs' leadership is its widespread adoption by major enterprises. An estimated 41–60% of Fortune 500 companies use ElevenLabs' technology in some capacity [4, 5]. While specific client lists are often confidential, the use cases span numerous industries:
- Customer Service: Powering AI agents that handle tens of thousands of support calls monthly, answering queries and resolving issues with human-like empathy.
- Media & Publishing: Automating the creation of audiobooks and news articles, with publishers like The Washington Post and Storytel using the platform to convert written content into engaging audio.
- Gaming: Generating dynamic, in-game character dialogue, allowing for more immersive and interactive storytelling.
- Healthcare: Creating personalised voice assistants for patients and even helping individuals who have lost their voice due to illness to communicate again, a mission highlighted in their "$1 Billion Effort" docuseries [6].

---
## Connecting the Power of ElevenLabs to Your Business: The Role of SIP Trunking
Having a powerful AI voice agent is one thing; connecting it reliably to the global telephone network is another. This is where Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) trunking becomes essential. A SIP trunk is a digital equivalent of a traditional phone line that uses an internet connection to link your company's phone system (your PBX) to the public telephone network.
Instead of running your AI agent in a silo, SIP trunking allows it to function as a fully-fledged member of your team. It can make and receive calls using your existing business phone numbers, transfer calls to human colleagues, and be managed within your central phone system.
Architecture diagram
[Image: Architecture diagram showing ElevenLabs AI Agent connecting via SIP/RTP to Click2Call SIP Trunk and then to inbound callers and human handoff]
How ElevenLabs connects to your business phone number via a Click2Call SIP trunk
### How Click2Call Bridges the Gap
Click2Call provides the critical infrastructure that makes this connection seamless and robust. By using a Click2Call SIP trunk, you can directly link your ElevenLabs conversational AI agent to our carrier-grade voice network. This offers several key advantages:
| Advantage | Why It Matters |
|---||---|
| Use Your Existing Numbers | There's no need to advertise new phone numbers. Your customers can continue calling the number they already know, and the call will be routed directly to your ElevenLabs agent. |
| Carrier-Grade Reliability | Our network is built for high-availability and call quality, ensuring that your AI agent is always online and that conversations are crystal clear, without the jitter or dropped calls common on lower-quality VoIP networks. |
| Scalability and Cost-Effectiveness | SIP trunks are far more flexible and cost-effective than traditional phone lines. You can easily scale the number of channels (simultaneous calls) up or down as your needs change, paying only for the capacity you require. |
| Unified Call Management | All calls, whether handled by a human or an AI agent, are managed through your Click2Call portal. This provides unified reporting, call recording, and analytics, giving you a complete picture of your business communications. |

ElevenLabs itself has recognised the importance of this integration, recently upgrading its SIP capabilities to make it easier for businesses to connect their agents to telephony providers like Click2Call [7]. This direct integration is the key to unlocking the full potential of voice AI within a professional business environment.
Placeholder for Setup Guide
### Step-by-Step Setup Guide Coming Soon
We are currently finalising a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to configure your ElevenLabs agent and connect it to your Click2Call account using a SIP trunk.
This guide will be published in our Help Centre shortly. Stay tuned!
---
## Conclusion: The Future of Business Communication is Here
ElevenLabs has established itself as the clear leader in voice AI not just through marketing hype, but through superior technology that delivers tangible results. Its ability to produce realistic, low-latency speech has made it the platform of choice for thousands of businesses, including a significant portion of the Fortune 500.
For Australian businesses, the path to leveraging this transformative technology is clear. By combining the intelligence of an ElevenLabs conversational AI agent with the reliability and scalability of a Click2Call SIP trunk, you can build a communications system that is more efficient, more insightful, and provides a vastly superior customer experience. The future isn't just calling; it's talking, and it sounds more human than ever before.

## How to Get a Business Phone Number: A Guide for New Businesses | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/how-to-get-a-business-phone-number
**Description:** Learn how to get a business phone number in Australia. Compare local numbers, 1300 numbers, and VoIP options for your business.

HEADER
END HEADER
HERO
[Image: A person starting a new business making a call on their smartphone in a modern office.]
New Business Guide
# How to Get a Business Phone Number
A practical guide for new Australian business owners on getting a local phone number, setting up a cloud phone system, and using an AI receptionist to handle calls from day one.
ARTICLE CONTENT
The Click2Call Blog
When you are starting a new business, your to-do list is a mile long. Between registering your ABN, building a website, and finding your first customers, setting up a phone system can feel like an afterthought. Yet, your phone is the most direct line to your customers. Getting it right from day one can be the difference between looking like a polished professional and looking like a hobbyist operating from their garage.
The problem is, you are flat out. You do not have time to answer every call, especially when half of them are telemarketers. You cannot afford to miss an important lead, but you also cannot afford to waste an hour on the phone with a time-waster. This is the classic new business dilemma.
This guide will walk you through the modern, efficient way to set up your business phone number. We will cover why a dedicated local number is crucial, how to get one in minutes, and how to use smart tools like an AI Receptionist to manage your calls — so you can focus on what actually matters: building your business.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 1. Why a Dedicated Business Number Matters
It is tempting to just use your personal mobile number when you first start. It is easy and it is free. But this is one of the most common mistakes new business owners make. Giving out your personal mobile number publicly creates problems you will not anticipate until it is too late.
A proper business number — whether it is a local landline number or a 1300 number — instantly makes your business look more established and trustworthy. A mobile number on a website or business card can signal that you are a very small, one-person operation, which might deter some larger clients or corporate buyers.
Once your mobile number is out there, it is out there forever. Do you really want customers calling you at 8 PM on a Sunday? Or worse, do you want your number scraped by marketing lists and spam callers? A separate business number allows you to set clear boundaries, define business hours, and route calls intelligently.
There is also the question of growth. What happens when you hire your first employee? You cannot share your personal mobile. A business number is an asset that belongs to the company. It can be diverted to different people, connected to a menu system, or have its own professional voicemail — completely independent of any single person's device.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 2. Local Landline vs. 1300 Number: Which is Right for You?
When you get a business phone number, you have two main choices: a geographic number (a local landline number with an area code) or a non-geographic number (like a 1300 number).
COMPARISON CALLOUT
Local Number (e.g. 07, 03, 08)
Best for businesses targeting a specific city or region. Builds local trust and community presence. Ideal for tradies, professional services, retail, and hospitality.
1300 Number
Best for national businesses wanting a corporate image. Callers pay a local call rate from anywhere in Australia. Better suited to established brands than startups.
For most new businesses targeting a specific city or region, a local number is the stronger choice. If you are a plumber in Perth, having a local (08) number builds immediate trust and relevance with Perth-based customers. It shows you are part of the local community, not some faceless national company. People are often more inclined to call a local number when searching for local services.
The good news is that with a modern Cloud PBX system, you can have a local landline number — like a Brisbane 07 number — that is not physically tied to a location. It lives in the cloud and can be answered anywhere in Australia.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 3. How to Get Your Business Phone Number in Minutes
Forget dealing with Telstra and waiting weeks for a technician. Getting a business phone number today is a simple online process that takes less than 10 minutes. Here is how it works:
Sign up for a Cloud PBX phone line
This is the engine of your phone system. It is a monthly plan that gives you the phone line itself and all the features — voicemail, call routing, AI tools, and more.
Choose your number
During signup, you can either select a brand new local number for your city (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, and more) or port an existing number you already own.
Activate and configure
Once you complete the signup, your new number is typically active within 24 hours — often much faster. It is now yours and ready to be configured in the portal.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 4. The Simple Setup: Diverting Calls to Your Mobile
Once your number is active, the simplest way to get started is to forward all calls directly to your mobile. This is a great starting point for a sole trader or a very small team.
In your phone system portal, you simply set the destination for your new business number to be your personal mobile number. When someone calls your business number, your mobile rings. You can answer it just like any other call. The caller does not know it has been diverted — they just know they have called your business.
This setup achieves the primary goal of separating your numbers while keeping you reachable. However, it still has one major drawback: you still have to answer every single call yourself.
TIP BOX
Good starting point, but not the end goal
Call diversion to mobile is perfect for your first week. Once you have your number working, consider setting up an AI Receptionist (see Section 5) to handle the volume as your business grows.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 5. The Smarter Setup: Your Own AI Receptionist
IN-ARTICLE IMAGE
[Image: A new business owner working at a desk while an AI receptionist handles an incoming call on a smartphone]
An AI Receptionist handles incoming calls automatically, sending you a transcript by email so you can decide who to call back.
This is where things get really powerful for a busy new business owner. Instead of just diverting calls to your mobile, you can place an AI Receptionist in front of them. Think of it as a virtual gatekeeper that filters all your calls for you — and it is included free with every Click2Call phone line.
Here is how it works in practice:
A customer calls your business number.
The AI Receptionist answers instantly with a professional greeting you have customised — for example, "Thanks for calling Brisbane Plumbing. How can I help you today?"
The caller states what they need in their own words. The AI understands their intent and either answers the question directly or takes a callback message.
Immediately after the call ends, you receive an email with a full transcript of the conversation and the audio recording.
HIGHLIGHT BOX
Included free with every Click2Call phone line
Most standalone AI receptionist services charge between $100 and $300 per month on top of your phone bill. With Click2Call, the AI Receptionist is built into every Cloud PBX phone line at no extra cost.
The power of this setup is immense. You are not interrupted by unimportant calls. You do not waste time answering basic questions. You get a perfect record of every enquiry, and you can quickly scan the email transcript to decide if it is an urgent lead that needs an immediate callback or something that can wait. You are in complete control, and you never miss an opportunity.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 6. Professional Voicemail and After-Hours Greetings
Whether you use a simple divert or an AI Receptionist, you need a professional voicemail message. The old way was to record a message yourself by talking into your phone — which often sounds muffled, rushed, or unprofessional. The modern way is to use AI Speech tools.
You simply type what you want your voicemail greeting to say, choose a voice actor, and the system generates a studio-quality audio file for you. This ensures your voicemail greeting is always clear, professional, and consistent — regardless of who set it up or when.
You can set up different greetings for different situations: a standard unavailable message, a separate after-hours message that plays outside your business hours, and even a seasonal greeting for periods like Christmas and Easter.
SECTION DIVIDER
## 7. Your Day One Phone System Checklist
Here is a simple, practical checklist to get your phone system sorted on day one of your new business.
Get a Cloud PBX phone line
Sign up online and choose your local business number for your city.
Choose your setup
Start with a simple call divert to your mobile, or go straight to the AI Receptionist. You can switch at any time.
Record your voicemail greeting
Use AI Speech to create a professional unavailable message in minutes.
Test it
Call your new business number from a different phone to make sure everything works exactly as you expect.
SECTION DIVIDER
## Conclusion
Setting up a professional business phone number is no longer a complex or expensive task. With a Cloud PBX system, you can get a local number, separate your personal and business life, and implement powerful tools like an AI Receptionist from your very first day in business.
By automating your call handling, you free up your most valuable resource — your time — to focus on growth, confident that you will never miss an important call.
CTA BOX
Ready to get started?
### Get your business phone number today
Free to create. No credit card required. Your local number is ready in minutes.
Create Your Free Account

END FOOTER

## How to Secure Your Business Communications | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/how-to-secure-business-communications
**Description:** Practical steps Australian businesses can take to protect their phone systems from common threats like toll fraud, phishing, and data breaches.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
Security
# How to Secure Your Business Communications
Practical steps Australian businesses can take to protect their phone systems from common threats like toll fraud, phishing, and data breaches.
By Royce Clark
Published on 29 Jan 2026
Article Body
As business communications move to the cloud, security becomes more important than ever. Your phone system is a gateway to sensitive customer and company data, making it a target for malicious actors. Here are practical, essential steps every Australian business should take to secure their communications.
## 1. Choose a Secure-by-Design Provider
Your first line of defence is your provider. A reputable Cloud PBX provider should have security built into the core of their platform. Look for:
- Australian Data Centres: Ensures your data is stored locally and subject to Australian privacy laws.
- Encryption: All call signalling (SIP/TLS) and media (SRTP) should be encrypted to prevent eavesdropping.
- Proactive Fraud Detection: The provider should have automated systems to detect and block suspicious activity, such as unusual call patterns indicative of toll fraud.

## 2. Enforce Strong Password Policies
This is the most basic but crucial step. Weak or reused passwords are the most common entry point for attackers. Implement a strict password policy for all users accessing the phone system portal and softphone apps:
- Complexity: Require a mix of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols.
- Length: Enforce a minimum length of 12 characters.
- Uniqueness: Do not allow users to reuse old passwords.
- Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): Where available, enable MFA for an extra layer of security.

## 3. Restrict International Calling
Toll fraud is one of the biggest risks, where attackers gain access to your system and make thousands of dollars worth of calls to premium international numbers. The simplest way to prevent this is to restrict international calling for users who don't need it. A good provider will allow you to enable or disable international calling on a per-user basis.
## 4. Train Your Staff to Spot Phishing Attempts
Attackers often target employees directly through phishing emails or "vishing" (voice phishing) calls to trick them into revealing their login credentials. Conduct regular training to help your team recognise the signs of a phishing attempt:
- Urgent or threatening language.
- Requests for sensitive information like passwords or financial details.
- Emails from unfamiliar senders or with suspicious links.
- Callers claiming to be from a trusted organisation (like your bank or a government agency) and asking for personal data.

Instil a culture of "when in doubt, check it out." Encourage staff to verify any suspicious requests through a separate, trusted communication channel.
## 5. Secure Your Network
The security of your office network is also critical. Ensure your Wi-Fi network is protected with a strong WPA2 or WPA3 password. If you have a dedicated hardware firewall, work with your IT provider to ensure it is configured correctly and that only necessary ports are open to the internet.
## Conclusion: A Layered Approach
Securing your business communications requires a layered approach. It starts with choosing a provider that prioritises security, like Click2Call, which offers end-to-end encryption and proactive fraud monitoring. It then extends to implementing strong internal policies and, most importantly, educating your team to be your human firewall. By taking these steps, you can significantly reduce your risk and ensure your communications remain private and secure.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
What is toll fraud?
Toll fraud, also known as telephony fraud, is the unauthorised use of a business's phone system to make long-distance or international calls. Attackers hack into the system and rack up huge bills, leaving the business liable for the cost.
Does using a softphone app on my mobile create a security risk?
As long as you use a strong, unique password for the app and keep your mobile device's operating system updated, using a softphone is very secure. Reputable providers ensure their apps use encrypted connections to protect your calls.
Author Bio
[Image: Royce Clark]
Written by
Royce Clark
Royce Clark has over 15 years of experience working in the telecommunications industry, specialising in VoIP systems. He is a Voice Engineer at Click2Call, helping Australian businesses design and deploy modern, reliable cloud phone systems.

## Microsoft Teams Phone vs Cloud PBX | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/teams-vs-cloud-pbx
**Description:** A balanced comparison of using Microsoft Teams with Direct Routing versus a dedicated Cloud PBX, helping you choose the right solution for your needs.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
Microsoft Teams
# Microsoft Teams Phone vs Cloud PBX: Which Is Right for Your Business?
A balanced comparison of using Microsoft Teams with Direct Routing versus a dedicated Cloud PBX, helping you choose the right solution for your needs.
By Royce Clark
Published on 20 Feb 2026
Article Body
Microsoft Teams has become the collaboration hub for many Australian businesses. But when it comes to making and receiving external phone calls, you have a choice: enable calling within Teams itself, or use a separate, dedicated Cloud PBX system. Both are modern VoIP solutions, but they cater to different needs. This article provides a balanced comparison to help you decide which is the best fit for your business.
## Option 1: Microsoft Teams Phone System
You can turn Microsoft Teams into your business phone system. This allows your staff to make and receive external calls directly from the Teams app on their computer or mobile. To do this, you need two things:
- A Microsoft Teams Phone license: This is an add-on to your standard Microsoft 365 subscription.
- A connection to the public phone network: You have two main ways to achieve this: Microsoft Calling Plans: You buy your phone numbers and call minutes directly from Microsoft. This is simple but can be expensive and less flexible. Direct Routing: You use a third-party provider, like Click2Call, to connect Teams to the phone network via a SIP trunk. This is far more cost-effective and offers greater flexibility and support.

## Option 2: A Dedicated Cloud PBX
A dedicated Cloud PBX is a complete, standalone phone system hosted by a provider like Click2Call. It offers a comprehensive suite of advanced telephony features and is managed through its own web portal and dedicated mobile/desktop apps. It operates entirely independently of Microsoft Teams.
## Comparison: Teams Phone vs. Cloud PBX
Let's compare the two options across key business considerations.
| Feature | Microsoft Teams Phone (with Direct Routing) | Dedicated Cloud PBX |
|---||---||---|
| User Experience | Unified experience inside a single app (Teams) that staff already use. | Requires a separate app for calls, but this app is purpose-built for telephony and often more intuitive for call handling. |
| Core Features | Good for basic calling, call transfer, and voicemail. | Extensive, enterprise-grade features: advanced call routing, multi-level auto-attendants, call queues, reporting, and analytics. |
| Advanced AI | Limited to Microsoft's own AI features (Copilot), which can be very expensive. | Providers like Click2Call offer integrated, practical AI tools like real-time transcription, sentiment analysis, and AI Receptionist as part of standard plans. |
| Cost | Requires a Teams Phone license per user, plus the cost of the Direct Routing service. Can be more expensive for advanced features. | Simple, all-inclusive per-user monthly fee. Often a lower total cost of ownership, especially for businesses needing advanced features. |
| Support | Support is split between Microsoft (for the Teams platform) and the Direct Routing provider (for connectivity). Can be complex to troubleshoot. | Single point of contact. Your Cloud PBX provider manages the entire end-to-end service, providing dedicated, expert support. |

## Who Should Choose Microsoft Teams Calling?
Teams Calling is a great fit for businesses that are deeply embedded in the Microsoft 365 ecosystem and whose primary need is to consolidate communications into a single app. If your staff live in Teams all day and your call handling needs are relatively simple (e.g., direct calls to staff members), the convenience of a unified platform can be a major benefit.
## Who Should Choose a Dedicated Cloud PBX?
A dedicated Cloud PBX is the better choice for businesses that have more complex call handling requirements or rely on their phone system as a critical tool for sales and customer service. If you need sophisticated call routing, detailed analytics, advanced AI features like an AI Receptionist, or have a contact centre environment, a dedicated Cloud PBX will provide a more powerful, reliable, and cost-effective solution.
## The Verdict
The choice is not about which is "better," but which is the "best fit" for your specific business needs. For ultimate simplicity and a unified user experience, Teams Calling is compelling. For ultimate power, features, and specialised telephony support, a dedicated Cloud PBX is the clear winner. At Click2Call, we offer both solutions, so we can help you make the right choice for your Australian business.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
Can I use physical desk phones with Microsoft Teams?
Yes, you can. However, you must use special "Teams-certified" desk phones, which are often more expensive than standard VoIP/SIP phones. A dedicated Cloud PBX offers a much wider range of compatible and affordable handset options.
Can I have both?
While technically possible, it is generally not recommended to run both systems in parallel for the same group of users as it can lead to a confusing user experience and unnecessary cost. It's better to choose the primary platform that best suits your business needs.
Author Bio
[Image: Royce Clark]
Written by
Royce Clark
Royce Clark has over 15 years of experience working in the telecommunications industry, specialising in VoIP systems. He is a Voice Engineer at Click2Call, helping Australian businesses design and deploy modern, reliable cloud phone systems.
