## VoIP for Small Business Australia: Setup Guide | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/voip-for-small-business
**Description:** A step-by-step guide for Australian small businesses on how to switch to a VoIP phone system, from choosing a provider to setting up your hardware.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
# VoIP for Small Business Australia: The Complete Setup Guide
A step-by-step guide for Australian small businesses on how to switch to a VoIP phone system, from choosing a provider to setting up your hardware.
By Royce Clark
Published on 19 Feb 2026
Article Body
Switching your small business to a VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol) phone system is one of the most impactful upgrades you can make. It cuts costs, increases flexibility, and provides a suite of professional features that were once only available to large enterprises. This guide will walk you through the entire process, step-by-step, for a smooth transition.
## Step 1: Assess Your Needs and Internet Connection
Before you start, take stock of your requirements:
- How many users do you have? This will determine the number of licenses you need.
- What features are essential? Do you need call recording, an auto-attendant (IVR), or CRM integration? Make a list of must-haves and nice-to-haves.
- Do you want to keep your existing phone number? Most providers offer number porting.

Most importantly, check your internet connection . VoIP relies on a stable, high-quality internet connection. Run a speed test to check your upload and download speeds and latency (ping). For reliable call quality, a business-grade NBN connection is strongly recommended.
## Step 2: Choose the Right VoIP Provider
Not all VoIP providers are created equal. Look for an Australian-based provider that offers:
- Transparent Pricing: Clear per-user, per-month pricing with no hidden fees. Check what's included in the monthly fee (e.g., standard calls, support).
- Local Support: Having access to an Australian support team who understands the local market is invaluable.
- Scalability: Choose a provider that makes it easy to add or remove users as your business grows or changes.
- A Strong Feature Set: Ensure the provider offers the features you identified in Step 1. A provider like Click2Call includes advanced features like AI-powered transcription and an AI Receptionist even in affordable plans.

## Step 3: Select Your Hardware (or Go App-Only)
One of the biggest benefits of VoIP is that you don't necessarily need traditional desk phones.
- Softphones (Apps): Most providers offer free desktop and mobile apps that turn your computer or smartphone into a fully-featured business phone. This is the most flexible and cost-effective option, perfect for remote teams.
- VoIP Desk Phones: If you prefer a physical handset, you'll need to purchase VoIP-compatible phones. Brands like Yealink and Grandstream are popular choices. Your provider can usually supply pre-configured phones for a plug-and-play experience.
- Headsets: A good quality USB or Bluetooth headset is essential for clear, hands-free communication, especially when using softphones.

## Step 4: The Onboarding and Porting Process
Once you've signed up with a provider, the setup process begins:
- Configuration: Your provider will work with you to configure your system. This includes setting up your auto-attendant ("Press 1 for Sales..."), voicemail, call groups, and business hours.
- Number Porting: You will need to sign a Porting Authority Form (PAF) to give your new provider permission to move your phone number from your old carrier. This process is managed entirely by the provider and can take 5-30 business days. Do not cancel your old service until porting is complete.
- Hardware Delivery: If you ordered desk phones, they will be shipped to you.

## Step 5: Go-Live and Training
On the scheduled "go-live" day, your number will be active on the new system. Here’s what to do:
- Plug in your phones: If you have desk phones, simply plug them into your office network. They should automatically connect to the system.
- Install the apps: Download the softphone apps onto your computers and mobiles and log in with the credentials provided.
- Test everything: Make some inbound and outbound calls to ensure everything is working as expected.
- Train your team: While modern VoIP systems are intuitive, a quick training session on how to use the new features (like call transfer, conferencing, and checking voicemail) will ensure a smooth adoption.

## Conclusion
Making the switch to VoIP is a straightforward process that delivers immediate benefits for Australian small businesses. By choosing a reputable local provider and following these steps, you can unlock a more powerful, professional, and cost-effective communication system that will grow with your business.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
Can I have a 1300 number with a VoIP system?
Absolutely. You can port your existing 1300 or 1800 number to your VoIP provider, or they can supply you with a new one. All incoming calls to your toll-free number will be routed through your new VoIP system.
What happens if my internet goes down?
This is a key advantage of VoIP. If your office internet fails, your provider can automatically forward all incoming calls to a designated backup number, such as a mobile phone. This ensures you never miss a customer call, even during an outage.

[Image: Royce Clark]
Written by
Royce Clark
Royce Clark has over 15 years of experience working in the telecommunications industry, specialising in VoIP systems. He is a Voice Engineer at Click2Call, helping Australian businesses design and deploy modern, reliable cloud phone systems.

## What Is a SIP Trunk? A Plain-English Guide | Click2Call Blog

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/blog/what-is-a-sip-trunk
**Description:** Understand what SIP trunks are, how they work, and why they are the modern, cost-effective replacement for traditional ISDN phone lines.

HEADER
/HEADER
Breadcrumb
Article Hero
SIP Trunks
# What Is a SIP Trunk? A Plain-English Guide for Australian Businesses
Understand what SIP trunks are, how they work, and why they are the modern, cost-effective replacement for traditional ISDN phone lines.
By Royce Clark
Published on 21 Feb 2026
Article Body
If you've been researching business phone systems in Australia, you've likely come across the term "SIP Trunk." It sounds technical, but the concept is quite simple. A SIP trunk is the modern, digital equivalent of a traditional phone line. It allows you to make and receive calls over the internet, replacing the need for outdated and expensive ISDN or PSTN lines. This guide explains everything you need to know in plain English.
## From ISDN to SIP: A Quick History
For decades, businesses relied on ISDN (Integrated Services Digital Network) to connect their on-premise PBX (phone system hardware) to the public telephone network. ISDN provided multiple digital channels, allowing businesses to make and receive several calls at once. However, with the rollout of the NBN, these old copper-based networks are being decommissioned. SIP is the technology that replaces them.
## What is SIP and How Does it Work?
SIP stands for Session Initiation Protocol . It's a set of rules that allows for the creation, modification, and termination of real-time communication sessions—like a phone call—over an internet connection. A "SIP Trunk" is the virtual link that connects your business's phone system (your IP PBX) to the wider telephone network via your internet connection.
Think of it like this: your IP PBX is your internal phone network, managing all your extensions and features. The SIP trunk is the multi-lane highway that connects your internal network to the outside world, allowing you to make and receive external calls.
## What are "Channels"?
A key concept in SIP trunking is the "channel." One channel is equivalent to one concurrent call. If you have 10 channels, your business can make or receive up to 10 calls at the same time. This is a major advantage over traditional lines, where you were locked into a fixed number of lines (e.g., 10, 20, or 30 with ISDN). With SIP, you can choose the exact number of channels you need and easily add or remove them as your business requirements change.
## The Benefits of SIP Trunking
For Australian businesses with an existing on-premise IP PBX, switching to SIP trunks offers several significant advantages:
- Cost Savings: SIP trunks are significantly cheaper than ISDN line rental. You can save up to 70% on your monthly phone bill by eliminating fixed line rental costs. Call rates are also generally much lower.
- Flexibility: Easily scale your channels up or down as needed. If you have a busy period, you can add more channels temporarily and then remove them afterwards, only paying for what you use.
- Business Continuity: If your office experiences an outage (e.g., power or internet failure), your SIP provider can automatically re-route all incoming calls to alternative numbers, such as mobile phones, ensuring you never miss a customer call.
- Unified Communications: SIP is the foundation for Unified Communications (UC), allowing you to integrate voice, video, and messaging into a single platform.
- Keep Your Numbers: You can port all your existing business phone numbers to your SIP trunking service, ensuring a seamless transition for your customers.

## Who is SIP Trunking For?
SIP trunking is the ideal solution for any Australian business that has already invested in an on-premise IP PBX system (from brands like 3CX, Avaya, Cisco, or Mitel) and wants to reduce costs and increase flexibility by moving away from traditional ISDN lines. If you don't have an existing IP PBX, a fully hosted Cloud PBX solution is likely a better fit, as it provides all the benefits of VoIP without needing any on-site hardware.
FAQ Section
## Frequently Asked Questions
How many channels do I need?
A good rule of thumb is one channel for every three to four employees. However, this can vary depending on your business type. A busy sales-focused business might need a higher ratio, while a standard office may need fewer. We can analyse your current call patterns to recommend the optimal number of channels.
Is SIP trunking reliable?
Yes. When delivered over a quality business-grade internet connection, SIP trunking is just as reliable as traditional phone lines. With built-in redundancy and failover options, it can often provide superior uptime and business continuity.
Author Bio
[Image: Royce Clark]
Written by
Royce Clark
Royce Clark has over 15 years of experience working in the telecommunications industry, specialising in VoIP systems. He is a Voice Engineer at Click2Call, helping Australian businesses design and deploy modern, reliable cloud phone systems.

     SECTION 2: KEY TAKEAWAYS

## Key Takeaways
- Click2Call's Cloud PBX is a fully hosted business phone system — all hardware, software, and infrastructure is managed by Click2Call, so businesses need nothing more than an internet connection and a device to make calls.
- Cloud PBX Landlines are $10.00/month (ex GST) per number. Direct Dial Numbers are $5.00/month. PBX Extensions are free — ideal for meeting rooms or staff who don’t need their own number. No bundled plans — pay only for what you use. No lock-in contracts and a 30-day free trial is available.
- The system includes 12+ enterprise features including auto-attendant (IVR), call recording, voicemail-to-email, call queues, ring groups, conference calling, and a mobile app for iOS and Android.
- Number porting is fully supported — Click2Call can port Australian geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08), mobile numbers, and 1300/1800 numbers with zero downtime during the switchover.
- Available to businesses across all of Australia on Australian-hosted infrastructure and local support from an Australian-based team.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 3: WHAT IS CLOUD PBX
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## What Is a Cloud PBX Phone System?
A Cloud PBX — also referred to as a hosted PBX, virtual PBX, or cloud phone system — is a business telephone exchange that runs entirely on servers in the cloud rather than on physical equipment installed at your office. The term PBX stands for Private Branch Exchange, which is the technology that routes calls between internal extensions and the public telephone network. In a traditional setup, this equipment sits in a server room or comms cabinet on your premises. With a hosted PBX , that same functionality is delivered as a service over the internet, managed entirely by your provider.
The shift to cloud telephony has been driven by the widespread availability of high-speed broadband across Australia. Because voice calls are transmitted as data packets over the internet — a technology known as business VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol) — there is no longer any need for dedicated telephone lines or expensive on-premise hardware. Your phone system becomes software: configurable, scalable, and accessible from anywhere with an internet connection.
The difference between a Cloud PBX and an on-premise system is significant in practical terms. An on-premise PBX requires a capital investment in hardware, professional installation, and ongoing maintenance by a qualified technician. Every time you need to add a new user, change a call routing rule, or add a feature, you typically need to call a technician or wait for a scheduled maintenance window. With hosted telephony , all of that is replaced by a web-based portal you control yourself. Adding a new seat takes seconds, and changes to your call routing take effect immediately.
Click2Call's Cloud PBX is built on Australian-hosted infrastructure, which means your voice traffic never leaves the country for domestic calls. This delivers lower latency, better call quality, and compliance with Australian data sovereignty requirements. Whether you are a sole trader wanting a professional phone presence or a multi-site business needing centralised call management, a cloud phone system from Click2Call gives you enterprise-grade capability at a fraction of the cost of traditional telephony.
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 4: FEATURES GRID
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## What Features Does Click2Call's Cloud PBX Include?
Every plan includes the full feature set. There are no feature tiers — you get everything from day one.
Auto-attendant / IVR
### Auto-attendant / IVR
Greet callers with a professional menu and route them to the right department automatically, 24/7.
Call Recording
### Call Recording
Record inbound and outbound calls automatically or on demand. Recordings are stored securely and accessible via the portal.
Voicemail to Email
### Voicemail to Email
Voicemail messages are transcribed and delivered to your inbox as an audio file and text, so you never miss a message.
Call Queues
### Call Queues
Hold callers in a queue with custom hold music and position announcements while your team handles other calls.
Ring Groups
### Ring Groups
Ring multiple team members simultaneously or in sequence until someone answers, ensuring no call goes unanswered.
Music on Hold
### Music on Hold
Upload your own music or promotional messages to play when callers are on hold, reinforcing your brand experience.
Call Transfer
### Call Transfer
Transfer calls to any extension, mobile number, or external number with a single click — blind or attended transfer supported.
Conference Calling
### Conference Calling
Host multi-party conference calls with internal and external participants directly from your business phone system.
Mobile App
### Mobile App
Use your business number on any iOS or Android device. Make and receive calls, transfer, and access voicemail on the go.
Web Portal
### Web Portal
Manage your entire phone system from any web browser. Add users, change routing rules, and view call reports in real time.
Number Porting
### Number Porting
Keep your existing business number. Click2Call manages the full porting process for geographic, mobile, and 1300 numbers.
Australian Numbers
### Australian Local & 1300 Numbers
Get new Australian geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08). Direct Dial Numbers from $5.00/month, full Cloud PBX Landlines from $10.00/month (all ex GST).
/grid
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 5: HOW IT WORKS
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## How Does Click2Call's Cloud PBX Work?
Getting started is straightforward. Most businesses are making and receiving calls on their new system within a single business day.
Step 1
### Sign Up Online
Create your Click2Call account at portal.click2call.com.au/join . Choose your plan, select the number of seats, and pick your phone numbers. The whole process takes about five minutes.
Step 2
### Configure Your System
Use the Click2Call portal to set up your IVR menus, ring groups, business hours, voicemail greetings, and call routing rules. Everything is point-and-click — no technical knowledge required.
Step 3
### Connect Your Devices
Download the Click2Call softphone app on your computer or mobile, or configure a compatible IP desk phone. Your team can start making and receiving calls immediately on their existing devices.
Step 4
### Port Your Number
If you are moving from an existing provider, Click2Call manages the number porting process end-to-end. Calls automatically switch over on the agreed porting date with zero interruption to your service.
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 6: WHO IS IT FOR
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Is Cloud PBX Right for Your Business?
Click2Call's Cloud PBX is designed to scale with your business, from a sole trader to a multi-site enterprise.
### Small Business (1–10 staff)
A small business does not need a large budget to sound like a large business. With a single Cloud PBX landline from $10/month (ex GST), you get a professional auto-attendant that greets callers, routes them to the right person, and sends missed calls straight to your email as a voicemail recording. Whether you are a sole trader in Brisbane or a small team in Sydney, you can project a polished, professional image from day one — without paying for hardware or signing a contract.
### Medium Business (10–50 staff)
If your business is outgrowing its current phone system, a Cloud PBX gives you the tools to handle higher call volumes without the cost of replacing on-premise hardware. Call queues keep customers informed while they wait, ring groups ensure the right team member answers, and call recording gives managers the visibility they need to coach staff and resolve disputes. Adding a new team member takes minutes in the portal — no technician required.
### Multi-site Business
Managing separate phone systems across multiple offices is costly and complicated. A Cloud PBX unifies all of your sites under a single system managed through one portal. Staff in Melbourne can transfer a call to a colleague in Perth as easily as if they were sitting across the room. You can assign local numbers to each office while maintaining a single, centralised call flow — giving customers a consistent experience regardless of which location they reach.
### Remote & Hybrid Teams
For teams that work from home, on the road, or across different time zones, a Cloud PBX removes the need for a physical office phone entirely. Staff use the Secure VoIP app on their mobile or desktop to make and receive calls on their business number — wherever they have an internet connection. Calls are routed through the same system as the rest of the team, so customers always reach the right person, and managers can see call activity across the whole team in real time.
### Startups
When you are building a business from scratch, every dollar counts. A Cloud PBX lets you launch with a professional phone presence from day one without any capital expenditure. There is no hardware to purchase, no installation fee, and no lock-in contract. Start with a single landline and a handful of free extensions, then add numbers, users, and features as your team grows. Because Click2Call operates on a prepaid credit model, you only ever pay for what you actually use.
### Regulated Industries
Businesses in financial services, legal, healthcare, and real estate often have strict obligations around record-keeping and communication. Click2Call's built-in call recording captures every inbound and outbound call and stores the recordings securely on Australian infrastructure. Detailed call logs provide a full audit trail, and recordings can be downloaded at any time from the portal. This makes it straightforward to meet compliance requirements without relying on third-party recording tools.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 7: COMPARISON TABLE
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Cloud PBX vs Traditional Phone System: What's the Difference?
A side-by-side comparison of the key differences between a traditional on-premise PBX and Click2Call's Cloud PBX.
| Feature / Consideration | Traditional PBX | Click2Call Cloud PBX |
|---||---||---|
| Upfront hardware cost | $3,000 – $30,000+ | $0 — no hardware required |
| Installation & setup | Days to weeks, requires a technician | Same day, self-service online |
| Ongoing maintenance | Regular technician visits, spare parts | Zero — fully managed by Click2Call |
| Scalability | Limited by hardware capacity, costly to expand | Unlimited — add seats in seconds |
| Remote working support | Difficult — requires VPN or extra hardware | Native — works on any device, anywhere |
| Feature updates | Manual firmware upgrades, often costly | Automatic — new features at no extra cost |
| Disaster recovery | Hardware failure = total outage | Automatic failover to mobile or backup number |
| Monthly cost (10 users) | $500 – $1,500+ (lines, maintenance, licences) | Scale to as many numbers as you need — from $10/month per line (ex GST) |
| Contract commitment | Typically 3–5 year hardware finance | Month-to-month, cancel any time |

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 7B: CLOUD PBX VS NBN VOICE
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Cloud PBX vs. NBN Voice: What’s the Right Choice?
Many Australian businesses are still using the basic voice service bundled with their NBN connection. While this works for simple inbound calls, it is not a phone system — it is just a phone line. A Cloud PBX gives you the full set of features that a growing business actually needs, without requiring any additional hardware or a separate internet connection.
| Feature | NBN Voice (Basic) | Click2Call Cloud PBX |
|---||---||---|
| Auto-attendant / IVR | — | Included |
| Multiple extensions | — | Unlimited free extensions |
| Call recording | — | Included |
| Voicemail to email | — | Included |
| Ring groups & call queues | — | Included |
| Mobile & desktop app | — | Secure VoIP App (iOS, Android, Windows, Mac) |
| Business hours routing | — | Included |
| AI Receptionist | — | Available add-on |
| Number portability | Tied to NBN provider | Port your existing number in |
| Contract commitment | Tied to NBN plan term | No lock-in, cancel any time |
| Monthly cost | Bundled (no itemised cost) | From $10/month per number (ex GST) |

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION 9: FAQ (above CTA)
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Cloud PBX Frequently Asked Questions
Straight answers to the questions Australian businesses ask most about Cloud PBX.
FAQ items
What is a Cloud PBX?
A Cloud PBX (Private Branch Exchange) is a business phone system hosted in the cloud rather than on physical hardware at your premises. All call routing, voicemail, IVR menus, and features are managed through an online portal, and calls are delivered over your internet connection using VoIP technology. There is no on-site equipment to purchase, install, or maintain.
How is Cloud PBX different from a traditional phone system?
A traditional PBX requires expensive on-site hardware, professional installation, and ongoing maintenance. A Cloud PBX eliminates all of that — there is no hardware to buy, no technician to book, and no maintenance costs. You manage everything through a web browser and pay only for the services you use via a simple prepaid credit balance. You also gain features like automatic failover, remote working support, and instant scalability that are difficult or impossible with traditional systems.
Do I need to buy any hardware for Cloud PBX?
No hardware is required. You can use the Click2Call softphone app on any computer, smartphone, or tablet. If you prefer physical desk phones, Click2Call is compatible with most standard IP desk phones, which you can purchase separately or source yourself. Click2Call does not require any proprietary hardware.
Can I keep my existing phone number with Click2Call?
Yes. Click2Call supports full number porting for Australian geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08), mobile numbers, and 1300/1800 numbers. The porting process is managed by Click2Call and typically takes 5–10 business days, with zero downtime during the switchover. You continue receiving calls on your existing number throughout the process.
How many users can I have on Click2Call's Cloud PBX?
There is no fixed upper limit. Click2Call's Cloud PBX scales from a single seat for a sole trader up to hundreds of seats for large enterprises. You can add or remove seats at any time through the portal, and you only pay for what you use, deducted from your prepaid account balance.
Is Click2Call's Cloud PBX available Australia-wide?
Yes. Click2Call provides Cloud PBX services to businesses across all of Australia, including metropolitan and regional areas. The service runs on Australian-hosted infrastructure with no international routing required for domestic calls. You can provision numbers for any Australian state or territory.
What internet connection do I need for Cloud PBX?
Each active voice call uses approximately 100 kbps of bandwidth. A standard NBN connection is more than sufficient for most businesses. For offices with many simultaneous calls, Click2Call recommends a dedicated business broadband connection with Quality of Service (QoS) configured to prioritise voice traffic. Our support team can advise on network requirements for your specific situation.
How long does it take to set up Cloud PBX?
Most businesses are fully operational within one business day of signing up. The sign-up process takes approximately five minutes, and the Click2Call portal guides you through configuring your IVR, ring groups, and user accounts. If you need assistance, the Australian support team is available to help at no extra charge.
Does Click2Call offer a Cloud PBX free trial?
Yes. Click2Call offers a 30-day free trial for new accounts. No credit card is required to start, and there is no lock-in contract. You can cancel at any time during or after the trial period. The trial gives you full access to all features so you can evaluate the system thoroughly before committing.
Can I use my mobile phone with Click2Call's Cloud PBX?
Yes. The Click2Call mobile app is available for iOS and Android devices. It gives you full access to your business phone system from your smartphone — you can make and receive calls on your business number, transfer calls, access voicemail, and use all system features just as you would from a desk phone or computer.
What happens if my internet goes down?
Click2Call includes automatic failover options. You can configure your system to divert incoming calls to a mobile number or alternative landline if your internet connection is unavailable. This ensures your business remains reachable even during an outage. Outbound calling can also be made from the mobile app using your mobile data connection.
Does Click2Call support number porting in Australia?
Yes. Click2Call handles the full number porting process on your behalf. We can port Australian geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08 prefixes), mobile numbers, and 1300/1800 numbers. The process typically takes 5–10 business days and is completed with zero downtime. You will continue receiving calls on your existing number right up until the port is completed.
Is Cloud PBX better than NBN Voice for my business?
For most businesses, yes. NBN Voice is a basic residential-grade service that provides a single line with minimal features. Click2Call's Cloud PBX gives you unlimited free extensions, an auto-attendant, call recording, ring groups, voicemail to email, and a mobile app — all for a comparable or lower monthly cost. You also gain the flexibility to add numbers and features as your business grows, without changing providers or purchasing new hardware.
How does the prepaid billing system work?
Click2Call operates on a prepaid credit system. You top up your account balance in the amount you choose (from as little as $10), and services are deducted from that balance as you use them — monthly number fees, call charges, and any add-ons. There are no monthly invoices, no direct debits, and no risk of unexpected bills. When your balance runs low, you will receive a notification to top up. Your account will not go into debt.
What is the AI Receptionist add-on?
The AI Receptionist is an optional add-on that uses artificial intelligence to answer inbound calls, greet callers, collect information, and route calls intelligently — without any human involvement. It can handle after-hours calls, qualify leads, take messages, and transfer to the right person or department. It is available as a monthly add-on deducted from your prepaid balance. See the
AI Receptionist page
for full details.
/faq-accordion
View all frequently asked questions →

## Switch to Click2Call | How the Changeover Works

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/compare
**Description:** Switching your business phone system to Click2Call is simple. Learn how number porting works, what the setup process involves, and how to get started today.

===================== HEADER =====================
HERO
No downtime. No lock-in. No hardware required.
# Switch to Click2Call
Moving your business phone system is simpler than you think. Keep your existing number, ditch the desk phones, and be up and running within days.
Create Your Free Account
Call Us: 1300 884 879
HERO IMAGE
[Image: Small business team making calls on laptops and mobile phones using Click2Call VoIP]
WHO IS THIS FOR
## Who Is This For?
Click2Call is built for Australian businesses that want a reliable, modern phone system without the complexity or cost of traditional phone lines. It suits any business that makes and receives calls as part of their day-to-day operation — from a sole trader working from home to a small office with a team of ten.
It is particularly well suited to businesses that are currently paying for a Telstra, Optus, or TPG landline and want to move to a system that works on their existing computers and mobile phones. There is no need to buy new hardware, install anything on your network, or wait for a technician to visit.
If your team works across multiple locations, from home, or on the road, Click2Call gives everyone access to the same phone system regardless of where they are. Calls come in on your business number and can be routed to any device, anywhere.
WHAT YOU NEED
## What You Need to Get Started
There are no special requirements. If you have a reliable internet connection and a device to make calls on, you are ready to go.
### Internet Connection
A standard NBN or business broadband connection is all you need. No dedicated phone lines required.
### A PC or Smartphone
Our free softphone app runs on Windows and Mac. The mobile app is available for iOS and Android.
### A Credit Card for Top-Up
Click2Call is prepaid. Load an initial credit of $20–$50 to cover your first month of calls and services.
STEP BY STEP
## The Setup Process, Step by Step
From the moment you sign up to the moment your first call comes in, here is exactly what happens.
### Create Your Account
Complete the signup form at portal.click2call.com.au/join . The form takes around five minutes. Your account will be activated within 24 hours, after which you will receive a confirmation email with your login details.
At signup, let us know whether you would like to self-configure your system using our Help Centre guides, or whether you would prefer our team to handle the setup for you.
### Add Your Phone Number
Once your account is active, you can add a phone number from the portal. If you are starting fresh, you can choose a new local number for your city or region. Phone numbers cost $10.00 per month (ex GST) for a Cloud PBX landline, or $5.00 per month for a Direct Dial number.
If you want to keep your existing business number, you will need to port it across from your current provider. The porting process is covered in detail in the next section.
### Set Up Your Users
Add each member of your team as a user in the portal. Extensions are free — there is no per-seat charge for adding users. Each user gets their own extension number and can make and receive calls independently.
You can configure call routing to send incoming calls to a specific user, a ring group (where multiple phones ring at once), or an auto-attendant menu. Our call flow guide walks you through the options step by step.
### Install the Apps
Download and install the Click2Call softphone on each team member's PC. The app is available for Windows and Mac and takes around two minutes to install. Log in with your portal credentials and you are ready to make calls.
The mobile app is available from the App Store and Google Play. It is best used for outbound calls when your team is away from their desk. For receiving inbound calls reliably on mobile, we recommend setting up call forwarding to each team member's actual mobile number.
### Start Making Calls
Once your account is topped up with credit and your number is active, you are ready to go. Make a test call from the softphone to confirm everything is working, then cancel your old phone service when you are ready.
If you are porting your existing number, wait until the port is confirmed complete before cancelling your old service. We will notify you by email when the port is finalised.
NUMBER PORTING
## Keeping Your Existing Number — How Porting Works
Number porting is the process of transferring your existing phone number from your current provider to Click2Call. It is a regulated process in Australia, which means all carriers are required to support it. We can port numbers from Telstra, Optus, TPG, Aussie Broadband, Vonex, and all other major Australian carriers.
The most important thing to understand about porting is that there is no downtime . Your number stays active on your current provider throughout the entire process. On the day the port completes, the number transfers to Click2Call seamlessly.
The porting process typically takes 5 to 10 business days from the time we lodge the request. We handle the paperwork — you just need to provide us with your current account details so we can submit the port request on your behalf.
### Number Porting Timeline
Day 1 — Sign up and account activation
Create your account. Activation within 24 hours. Add a temporary new number to start using the system immediately if needed.
Day 1–3 — Port request lodged
We lodge the port request with your current carrier. Your existing number remains fully active throughout this period.
Day 5–10 — Port completes
Your number transfers to Click2Call. You receive a confirmation email. Calls now ring on your new system. No interruption to service.
After port — Cancel your old service
Once the port is confirmed, contact your old provider to cancel your line. Do not cancel before the port is complete.
Important: Do not cancel your existing phone service before the port is confirmed complete. Cancelling early can cause the port request to fail and may result in losing your number permanently.
PC APP VS MOBILE
## PC App vs Mobile App — What Works Best
Click2Call provides a free app for both PC and smartphone. Understanding how each works will help you set up your team in the way that suits them best.
### PC Softphone App
The PC app is our recommended solution for office-based staff. It is reliable for both inbound and outbound calling and integrates cleanly with your desktop workflow. Calls ring directly on screen, and you can manage transfers, hold, and call recording from the app interface.
Works on Windows and Mac. A USB headset is recommended for the best call quality, though the built-in microphone and speakers on most laptops work fine for everyday use.
### Mobile App
The mobile app works well for outbound calls when your team is away from the office. However, we recommend against relying on it as the primary way to receive inbound calls. Push notifications on smartphones are not always reliable, which means incoming calls can be missed.
This is not a limitation specific to Click2Call — it is a general limitation of SIP apps on mobile operating systems. For reliable inbound call handling on mobile, use call forwarding instead.
### Best Practice: Call Forwarding for Mobile Staff
The most reliable way to receive calls on a mobile phone is to route inbound calls directly to the staff member's actual mobile number. When a call comes in on your business number, Click2Call forwards it to the mobile number of your choice. The call rings just like a regular mobile call — no app required.
This approach gives your team the flexibility to work from anywhere without missing calls. Your standard per-minute call rate applies when calls are answered via forwarding. See our call flow setup guide for instructions.
SETUP OPTIONS
## Setup Options
You have two ways to get set up. Choose the option that suits your team and your level of technical confidence.
### Self-Setup
Free
Configure your own system using our step-by-step Help Centre guides. Each guide covers a specific task — activating your account, adding phone numbers, setting up users, configuring call routing, and installing the softphone.
Most customers complete the full setup in under an hour. If you get stuck at any point, our support team is available by phone and email to help.
View Help Centre guides
Recommended for busy teams
### Managed Setup
$300 + GST
Our team configures everything for you. This includes setting up all user accounts, configuring your call routing and ring groups, installing and testing the PC softphone on each workstation, and setting up the mobile app on your team's phones.
We also lodge your number port request and monitor it through to completion. Once everything is set up and tested, we hand over a brief guide specific to your configuration.
One-off fee. Covers up to 3 users. Additional users are $50 + GST each. Indicate your preference at signup.
REAL WORLD EXAMPLE
## A Real-World Example
To give you a concrete idea of what it costs to switch, here is a typical scenario for a small office moving from a Telstra landline to Click2Call.
Scenario: Small office, 3 users, 1 existing Telstra number to port
PC softphone for desk use, mobile app for outbound calls when out of office
Cloud PBX landline number (monthly)
$10.00 / month
Extensions for 3 users
Free
Number port from Telstra (one-off)
$50.00
Initial account credit top-up
$50.00
Total to get fully operational
Under $120
All prices ex GST. Ongoing cost is $10/month for the number plus call charges at pay-as-you-go rates from $0.10/min.

## Frequently Asked Questions
How long does it take to switch to Click2Call?
Your account is activated within 24 hours of signing up. If you are keeping your existing number, the port typically takes 5 to 10 business days. You can start making and receiving calls on a new number the same day your account is activated — you do not need to wait for the port to complete before using the system.
Will there be any downtime when I port my number?
No. Your existing number stays active on your current provider throughout the entire porting process. On the day the port completes, the number transfers to Click2Call seamlessly. There is no gap in service — calls that were going to your old provider will now ring on your Click2Call system instead.
Can I port my Telstra, Optus, or TPG number to Click2Call?
Yes. We can port numbers from all major Australian carriers including Telstra, Optus, TPG, Aussie Broadband, Vonex, and others. This includes local geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08) and 1300 numbers. If you are unsure whether your number can be ported, call us on
1300 884 879
and we will confirm.
Do I need to buy any hardware?
No hardware is required. Click2Call works on your existing PC via the softphone app and on your existing smartphone via the mobile app. If you prefer a physical desk phone, we support SIP-compatible handsets, but they are entirely optional. A USB headset is a useful addition for staff who spend a lot of time on calls, but is not required.
What is the difference between self-setup and managed setup?
Self-setup is free. Our Help Centre guides you through every step including activating your account, adding phone numbers, setting up users, and configuring call routing. Most customers complete the full setup in under an hour. Managed setup costs $300 + GST as a one-off fee. Our team configures everything for you including user accounts, call routing, mobile apps, and PC softphone installation.
How much does it cost to get started?
There are no setup fees for self-setup. A typical small office with one phone number and three users would pay $10/month for the number, plus a one-off $50 porting fee if keeping an existing number, plus an initial credit top-up of $50 to cover calls. That is under $120 to get fully operational. All prices are ex GST. See our
pricing page
for a full breakdown.
Can I use the mobile app to receive calls?
The mobile app works well for outbound calls. For inbound calls, we recommend routing calls to your actual mobile number rather than relying on the app. Push notifications on smartphones are not always reliable, which can cause missed calls. Forwarding to your mobile number means calls ring just like a normal call — no app required. Your standard per-minute rate applies when calls are answered this way.
Is there a lock-in contract?
No. Click2Call is entirely month-to-month with no lock-in contracts. You can add or remove phone numbers, users, and channels at any time from the portal. If you decide to leave, simply cancel from the portal — there are no cancellation fees.
What happens to my existing phone service during the switchover?
Your existing phone service continues to operate normally throughout the entire switchover process. You can run both systems simultaneously — your old service and your new Click2Call system — until you are confident everything is working correctly. Only cancel your old service after the port is confirmed complete and you have tested your new system.
Can I add more users or phone numbers later?
Yes. You can add phone numbers, users, extensions, and channels at any time from the portal. There is no limit on the number of users — extensions are free. Additional phone numbers are $5–$10 per month depending on the number type. Additional channels (concurrent call capacity) are $3.00 per month each.
What support is available if I get stuck?
Our Australian support team is available by phone on
1300 884 879
and by email at
support@click2call.com.au
. We also have a full
Help Centre
with step-by-step guides covering every aspect of the setup process.
Can I port a 1300 number to Click2Call?
Yes. We support porting of 1300 numbers from other Australian carriers. The one-off porting fee for a 1300 number is $100 + GST. The porting timeline is the same as for local numbers — typically 5 to 10 business days. Once ported, your 1300 number will ring on your Click2Call system just like any other number.
## Ready to Make the Switch?
Create your free account today. No credit card required to sign up. Our team is available to answer any questions before you commit.
Create Free Account
Call 1300 884 879
Or email us at support@click2call.com.au

── Header JavaScript ──
  Paste this <script> block just before <script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.faq-toggle').forEach(function(btn) {
        btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
            var expanded = this.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true';
            this.setAttribute('aria-expanded', !expanded);
            document.getElementById(this.getAttribute('aria-controls')).classList.toggle('hidden');
            this.querySelector('.faq-icon').style.transform = expanded ? '' : 'rotate(180deg)';
        });
    });
});
</script>
</body> on each page,
  or consolidate into a shared site.js file.

## VoIP & Cloud PBX FAQs Australia | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/faq
**Description:** Answers to the most common questions about Click2Call Cloud PBX, VoIP, AI voice tools, and SIP trunks. Pricing, setup, porting, and more.

============================================================
  Click2Call — Shared Header Snippet
  File: _header.html
  Usage: Copy everything between the <header> tags (inclusive)
         into each page, immediately after <body>.
  Active state: Add class "c2c-nav-active" to the <a> or
                <button> that matches the current page.
  Tailwind CSS must be loaded in the page <head>:
  ============================================================
===================== HEADER =====================
=================== END HEADER ===================
── Header JavaScript ──
  Paste this <script> block just before 
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    document.querySelectorAll(".faq-toggle").forEach(function(btn) {
        btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
            var expanded = this.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true';
            this.setAttribute('aria-expanded', !expanded);
            var answer = document.getElementById(this.getAttribute('aria-controls'));
            answer.classList.toggle('hidden');
            this.querySelector(".faq-icon").style.transform = expanded ? '' : 'rotate(180deg)';
        });
    });
    document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e) {
        if (e.key === 'Escape') {
            document.querySelectorAll(".faq-toggle[aria-expanded="true"]").forEach(function(btn) {
                btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
                document.getElementById(btn.getAttribute('aria-controls')).classList.add('hidden');
                btn.querySelector(".faq-icon").style.transform = '';
            });
        }
    });
});
</script>

</body> on each page,
  or consolidate into a shared site.js file.
SECTION 1: HERO
# Frequently Asked Questions
Find answers to the most common questions about our services. If you can't find what you're looking for, please contact us .
## Key Takeaways
- This page answers the most common questions about Click2Call's services, pricing, and technical requirements.
- Click2Call offers Cloud PBX, AI Voice Tools, AI Receptionist, SIP Trunks, and Microsoft Teams Calling.
- All services are prepaid with no lock-in contracts. A 30-day free trial is available for new accounts.
- Services are available to any Australian business with a stable internet connection.
- Call 1300 884 879 or email support@click2call.com.au for personalised advice.

FAQ Categories
- About Click2Call
- Cloud PBX & VoIP
- AI Tools
- Pricing & Contracts
- Setup & Support

## About Click2Call
What is Click2Call?
Click2Call is an Australian business communications provider, specialising in AI-enhanced Cloud PBX, VoIP, and SIP Trunking services. We provide modern, reliable, and cost-effective phone systems for businesses of all sizes across Australia.
Who is Click2Call for?
Our services are designed for Australian small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) looking to upgrade from traditional phone lines to a modern, feature-rich cloud-based system. We serve a wide range of industries, from professional services and trades to healthcare and retail.
Where does Click2Call operate?
Click2Call is a fully Australian-owned and operated company. We provide services to businesses in every state and territory, including all major cities like Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, and Adelaide, as well as regional and rural areas.
Is Click2Call an Australian company?
Yes, Click2Call is 100% Australian owned and operated. Our team is based in Australia, and our primary infrastructure is hosted in Australian data centres, ensuring low latency and data sovereignty for our customers.
What makes Click2Call different from other VoIP providers?
Our key differentiators are our integrated AI Voice Tools, our commitment to local Australian support, and our simple, transparent pricing with no lock-in contracts. We focus on making enterprise-grade technology accessible and easy to use for any Australian business.
What is Bcom Services Pty Ltd?
Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108) is the legal entity that owns and operates the Click2Call brand. Click2Call is our trading name for our suite of business communication services.
How long has Click2Call been in business?
Click2Call is operated by Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108), an Australian telecommunications company dedicated to making enterprise-grade cloud communications accessible to businesses of all sizes across Australia. We developed Click2Call to give Australian businesses modern, flexible phone systems without the complexity or cost of traditional hardware.
Does Click2Call have a physical office?
As a cloud-native company, our team operates in a hybrid model. Our primary customer-facing operations are managed through our online portal and our Australian-based support team, which you can reach by phone or email.
## Cloud PBX & VoIP
What is a Cloud PBX?
A Cloud PBX (Private Branch Exchange) is a business phone system that is hosted in the cloud by a provider like Click2Call. It delivers all the features of a traditional on-premise PBX — like call transfers, auto-attendants, and voicemail — over your internet connection, without the need for any bulky hardware.
How is Cloud PBX different from a traditional phone system?
The main difference is that a Cloud PBX is hosted off-site and managed by your provider, eliminating hardware costs and maintenance. It is also more flexible, scalable, and feature-rich than a traditional system, which is tied to physical phone lines and requires a technician for changes.
Do I need to buy any hardware for Cloud PBX?
No, you do not need to buy any specific PBX hardware. You can use our service with a range of devices, including existing VoIP desk phones, our mobile app (for iOS and Android), or a softphone application on your computer with a headset.
Can I keep my existing phone number?
Yes, absolutely. We can port your existing local, 1300, or 1800 numbers to our service with zero downtime. This process is called number porting and is included free of charge with our plans.
How many users can I have on Click2Call's Cloud PBX?
Our Cloud PBX is highly scalable and can support anywhere from a single user to thousands of users. You can easily add or remove users at any time through our online portal as your business needs change.
What internet connection do I need for VoIP?
VoIP itself does not use much data (about 100kbps per call). The most important factor is the stability and quality of your internet connection. A standard business-grade NBN, fibre, or reliable fixed wireless connection is typically sufficient.
What happens if my internet goes down?
A key benefit of our Cloud PBX is call continuity. If your office internet connection fails, our system can automatically forward all incoming calls to a designated mobile number or another location, so you never miss an important call.
Can I use my mobile phone with the Cloud PBX?
Yes. Our mobile app for iOS and Android turns your smartphone into a full-featured extension of your office phone system. You can make and receive calls using your business number, transfer calls to colleagues, and check voicemail from anywhere.
## AI Voice Tools & AI Receptionist
What are AI Voice Tools?
Our AI Voice Tools are a suite of features that use artificial intelligence to analyse your phone calls. This includes real-time transcription, automated call summaries, sentiment analysis (detecting positive or negative tone), and keyword detection for compliance and training.
How does the AI Receptionist work?
The AI Receptionist is a conversational AI that can answer incoming calls 24/7. It understands natural language, so callers can simply state who they want to speak to or what they need. It can then route the call, answer frequently asked questions, take a message, or even book an appointment.
Is the AI Receptionist just a fancy IVR menu?
No, it is far more advanced. An IVR (Interactive Voice Response) menu forces callers to navigate rigid options by pressing keys. Our AI Receptionist uses Natural Language Understanding (NLU) to have a human-like conversation, allowing callers to speak naturally.
How accurate is the call transcription?
Our transcription service is specifically trained on Australian accents and terminology, achieving over 95% accuracy in typical business conversations. This makes the transcripts a reliable record for compliance, training, and reference.
Can the AI tools integrate with my CRM?
Yes. We offer integrations with popular CRM platforms. This allows you to automatically log call recordings, transcripts, and summaries against customer records, providing a complete view of all communications.
Is the AI Receptionist available 24/7?
Yes. The AI Receptionist can be configured to answer all calls, only calls outside of business hours, or only when your team is busy and unable to answer. This ensures your business never misses a call, day or night.
What languages does the AI support?
Currently, our AI tools are optimised for Australian English. We are continuously working on expanding our language support based on customer demand.
How secure is the data from the AI tools?
All call data, including recordings and transcripts, is encrypted both in transit and at rest. We adhere to strict data privacy and security protocols, and all data is stored within our Australian data centres.
## Pricing & Contracts
How much does Click2Call cost?
Click2Call uses simple pay-as-you-go pricing — no bundled plans. Cloud PBX Landlines are $10.00/month (ex GST). Direct Dial Numbers are $5.00/month (ex GST). Microsoft Teams Lines are $10.00/month (ex GST). PBX Extensions are free. Every account includes 2 channels; additional channels are $3.00/month each. Call rates from $0.10/min for Australian and NZ fixed calls. All prices ex GST. No lock-in contracts.
Is there a setup fee?
No, we do not charge any setup fees. Our team will help you get your account configured and your numbers ported over at no extra cost.
Is there a lock-in contract?
No. Click2Call operates on a prepaid credit model with no lock-in contracts. You can add or remove services at any time and cancel whenever you like — there are no cancellation fees or notice periods.
Can I add or remove services after signing up?
Yes. You can add or remove phone numbers, channels, and other services at any time through the customer portal. Changes take effect immediately and are reflected in your prepaid balance — there are no billing cycles to wait for.
Does Click2Call offer a free trial?
Yes, we offer a 30-day free trial for new customers. This allows you to test our platform with your team and ensure it is the right fit for your business before committing.
What payment methods are accepted?
You top up your account credit using a major credit card (Visa, MasterCard, American Express) or direct debit from an Australian bank account. There are no monthly invoices — you add credit when you need it, starting from as little as $10. All charges are deducted directly from your prepaid balance.
Are call costs included?
No. Click2Call does not bundle calls into a plan. All calls are charged on a per-minute basis and deducted from your prepaid account balance. Australian and NZ fixed calls are $0.10/min, and calls to Australian mobiles are $0.20/min (all ex GST). Inbound calls are free.
Are prices shown ex GST?
Yes. All prices listed on our website are shown exclusive of GST (ex GST). GST of 10% is added when you top up your account credit.
## Setup, Support & Technical Requirements
How do I set up my Click2Call service?
Setup is simple and guided by our team. After you sign up, we will work with you to configure your call flows, users, and auto-attendant. We then help you port your numbers, and once that is complete, your service is live.
What kind of support does Click2Call offer?
We offer unlimited support from our Australian-based team. You can reach us by phone, email, or by logging a ticket through our customer portal during business hours.
What are your support hours?
Our standard support hours are Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM AEST. We maintain 24/7 network monitoring for critical system outages. For non-urgent issues outside business hours, please log a support ticket via the customer portal at portal.click2call.com.au/login.php
Do I need a special internet connection?
You do not need a special connection, but a stable, business-grade internet service is highly recommended for the best call quality. We advise against using residential-grade or mobile 4G/5G connections as the primary internet source for an office environment.
Can I use my existing desk phones?
If you have existing VoIP/SIP-compatible desk phones from brands like Yealink, Grandstream, or Cisco, they will likely work with our system. Our support team can verify compatibility for you.
How long does it take to port my phone number?
Number porting times can vary depending on the carrier you are moving from. Local number ports typically take 5-10 business days, while 1300/1800 number ports can take up to 30 days. We manage this entire process for you.
Do you provide on-site installation?
Our system is designed for remote setup and management, so on-site installation is generally not required. However, for large or complex deployments, we can connect you with one of our trusted installation partners in your area.
Where can I find help guides and documentation?
You can find a comprehensive knowledge base with setup guides, user manuals, and troubleshooting articles within the Click2Call customer portal.
## Still Have Questions?
Our Australian-based team is here to help. Get in touch to discuss your business needs.
Contact Us
Call 1300 884 879

## Activating Your Account | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-activate-account
**Description:** Learn what to expect after signing up to Click2Call and how to log in to your portal for the first time.

===================== HEADER =====================
Article header
Breadcrumb
Getting Started
5 min read
# Activating Your Account
This guide walks you through what happens after you complete the Click2Call signup form and how to access your portal for the first time.
Article body
Steps
Step 1
### Sign up for a free trial
Visit portal.click2call.com.au/join and complete the signup form. You will need to provide your name, email address, and a password. No credit card is required to start your 30-day free trial.
After submitting the form, check your inbox for a confirmation email from Click2Call. Click the verification link to activate your account before logging in.
Step 2
### Log in to your portal
Go to portal.click2call.com.au and enter your email address and password, then click Log in .
[Image: Click2Call portal login screen showing email and password fields]
The Click2Call portal login screen
Step 3
### Review your Account Summary
After logging in you will land on the Account Summary page. This shows your account name, account number, next billing date, current balance, and your active phone services. During your free trial, your DIY Core plan will be listed here.
[Image: Account Summary page showing account name, balance, and active phone services]
The Account Summary page — your starting point after login
Step 4
### Update your account details
Click Details in the left sidebar to open the Account Details page. Fill in your personal details, service address, and contact information. This information is used for billing and support. Click Update Details to save your changes.
[Image: Account Details page showing personal details, service address, and 2FA settings]
The Account Details page — update your contact and billing information here
We recommend enabling Two Factor Authentication (2FA) on this page to keep your account secure. You can choose to be prompted once per location for convenience.
Step 5
### Review your plan and channels
Click Plan in the left sidebar to view your current plan details. You will see your active services, included channels, and any line plans assigned to your numbers. Your account starts with 2 free channels (simultaneous call lines) by default.
[Image: Account Plan page showing current plan, line plans, and channel settings]
The Account Plan page — view your services, channels, and line plans
Step 6
### Start adding your phone services
Click Voice in the top navigation bar to go to the Voice Numbers page. From here you can add phone numbers, create extensions for your team, and provision desk phones and softphones. Use the buttons at the top — Add Phone Number , Add Extension , and Add Channels — to get started.
[Image: Voice Numbers page showing Add Phone Number, Add Extension, and Add Channels buttons]
The Voice Numbers page — your hub for managing extensions and phone numbers
Your account is now active and ready to use. Follow the guides below to add your first phone number, set up extensions for your team, and configure your call flows.
Related articles

Adding a New Extension
Adding a Phone Number (DDI)

## How to Add Account Credit | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-add-account-credit
**Description:** Step-by-step guide to adding account credit to your Click2Call pre-pay account via the Payments page.

===================== HEADER =====================
Breadcrumb
Article Header
Getting Started
3 min read
# How to Add Account Credit
Click2Call operates on a pre-pay system . Before you can make calls, add phone numbers, or use paid features, you need to load credit into your account. This guide walks you through the Account → Payments page and explains every element.
Info box
### How the pre-pay system works
- Your account balance is charged for monthly line fees, call usage, and any add-ons.
- You can set up auto top-up so your account never runs out of credit.
- All prices are shown in AUD ex GST .
- Minimum top-up amount is $10.00 .

Steps
Step 1
## Log in and check your Account Summary
Go to portal.click2call.com.au and sign in. After logging in you will land on the Account Summary page. This page shows your current balance at a glance.
[Image: Account Summary page showing account balance and Phone Services breakdown]
Element table
| Element | Description |
|---||---|
| Account Balance | Your total credit balance including any pending charges. |
| Available Balance | The amount available to spend after reserving funds for your next bill date. |
| Next Bill Date | The date your monthly line fees will next be deducted from your balance. |
| Phone Services table | A breakdown of your active services, their unit prices (ex. GST), and total monthly cost. |
| Add Credit Now button | A shortcut button that takes you directly to the Payments page to top up your balance. |

Step 2
## Navigate to Account → Payments
Click Payments in the left sidebar under the Account section, or click the yellow Add Credit Now button on the Account Summary page. Both take you to the same Payments page.
[Image: Account Payments page showing Stored Card Details and Notification Settings sections]
Step 3
## Understanding the Payments page
The Payments page is divided into two sections: Stored Card Details and Notification Settings . Here is a full explanation of every element.
### Stored Card Details
| Element | Description |
|---||---|
| Card number field | Enter your Visa or Mastercard credit or debit card number here. The portal uses Stripe for secure card processing — your card details are never stored on Click2Call servers. |
| Save Card button | Saves your card details securely for future payments and auto top-ups. You must save a card before enabling automatic payments. |
| Make a One Off Payment button | Charges your saved card immediately for the amount shown in the Auto Top-up Amount field. Use this when you want to manually add credit without setting up automatic top-ups. |
| Enable automatic payments | When ticked, the portal will automatically charge your saved card on your monthly bill date to cover your line fees. |
| Enable automatic top-ups | When ticked, the portal will automatically top up your account when your available balance falls below a threshold. This prevents service interruptions during high-usage periods. |
| Auto Top-up Amount | The dollar amount (AUD inc. GST) to charge your card each time an automatic top-up is triggered. Minimum value is $10.00. |
| Store Card Details button | Saves your automatic payment preferences (checkboxes and top-up amount) after you have made changes. |
| Visa / Mastercard logos | Indicates the accepted card types. Both Visa and Mastercard credit and debit cards are accepted. |

### Notification Settings
| Element | Description |
|---||---|
| Enable low credit warning emails | When ticked, the portal will send an email to the account administrator when your balance falls below the threshold you set. Recommended if you are not using auto top-up. |
| Credit amount threshold | The balance level (in AUD) at which a low credit warning email is sent. For example, entering $10.00 will trigger a warning email when your balance drops below $10.00. |
| Update button | Saves your notification settings after making changes. |

Step 4
## Add credit to your account
To add credit, follow these steps:
- Enter your card number in the card field (Visa or Mastercard).
- Click Save Card to securely store your card details.
- Set your desired Auto Top-up Amount (e.g. $20.00).
- Click Make a One Off Payment to immediately add that amount to your balance, or tick Enable automatic top-ups and click Store Card Details to set up recurring top-ups.

Tip: We recommend enabling both automatic top-ups and low credit warning emails to ensure your service is never interrupted.
Success tip
### ✓ Credit added — you are ready to go
With credit in your account you can now add phone numbers, make calls, and use all platform features. Next steps:
- Add a phone number to start receiving calls
- Add an extension for each team member
- Set up a call flow to route inbound calls

Related articles
### Related articles
Activating Your Account First steps after signing up
How to Add a Phone Number Add a Sydney, Melbourne or Brisbane number
===================== FOOTER =====================

## Adding Additional Channels | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-add-channels
**Description:** How to add more simultaneous call channels to your Click2Call account to handle higher call volumes.

===================== HEADER =====================
Page header
# How to Add More Phone Lines (Channels)
Increase your call capacity by adding more channels to your account, allowing more staff to be on the phone at the same time.
What are channels explanation
## What are Channels?
Think of a channel as a single phone line. If you have two channels , your business can make or receive a maximum of two calls at the same time . If a third call comes in while both channels are busy, that caller will go to voicemail or hear a busy signal.
Every Click2Call account comes with 2 free channels by default. You also receive one extra free channel for every purchased phone number on your account (excluding DDI/virtual numbers).
### How Many Channels Do I Need?
The number of channels you need depends on how many people will be on the phone simultaneously. A good rule of thumb for a typical office is to have 1 channel for every 3 to 4 users . For example, a business with 10 staff members would likely need around 3 or 4 channels to avoid congestion.
### How Much Do They Cost?
If you need more channels than are included with your plan, you can add them at any time. Additional channels cost $3.00 +GST per channel, per month . The cost is pro-rated for the first month.
Steps
### Navigate to the Plan Page
Log in to the portal, click the Account tab, and then select Plan from the left-hand navigation menu.
[Image: Navigate to the Plan page under the Account tab.]
### Select the Channels Tab
On the Plan page, click the Channels tab to view your current channel count and make changes.
[Image: Select the Channels tab on the Plan page.]
### Choose the Total Number of Channels
From the dropdown menu, select the total number of channels you require for your account. The system will automatically calculate how many new channels are being added and the associated cost.
[Image: Select the total number of channels required from the dropdown menu.]
### Update and Confirm
Review the ongoing and pro-rata costs for the new channels, then click the Update Channels button to confirm. The new channels will be active immediately.
[Image: Confirm the new channel cost and click Update Channels.]
===================== FOOTER =====================

---

## Adding & Provisioning a Desk Phone | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-add-desk-phone
**Description:** How to register and provision a physical desk phone (Yealink, Cisco, Grandstream, etc.) to your Click2Call account.

===================== HEADER =====================
Article header
Breadcrumb
Phones & Devices
4 min read
# Adding & Provisioning a Desk Phone
This guide covers two methods for connecting a physical desk phone to your Click2Call account. If you have a Yealink phone, follow the Auto Provisioning steps below for the fastest setup. For all other brands, skip to the Manual SIP Setup section.
Article body
===== SECTION 1: Yealink Auto Provisioning =====
## Option 1 — Yealink Auto Provisioning
Recommended for Yealink phones — fastest setup method
Click2Call has a fully automated Yealink device provisioning process to take the hard work out of configuring these phones. Follow the steps below to get your Yealink phone registered and ready to use in minutes.
### Unbox and assemble the phone
When opening your Yealink phone box, you will find the main phone unit, one handset cord, one handset, one phone stand, and one Ethernet cable. Flip the phone over and attach the stand to the back slots above the cable ports. Connect the larger end of the handset cord into the port marked with a phone icon on the unit, and the smaller end into the handset.
[Image: Yealink phone back panel showing the Internet port, PC port, handset port, and MAC address label]
### Connect to your network and power on
Plug one end of the Ethernet cable into the port labelled Internet on the back of the phone, and the other end into your internet modem or router. Once connected, the phone will power on and display a welcome message on screen.
[Image: Yealink phone back panel showing the Internet port where the Ethernet cable is connected]
### Add the phone in the Click2Call portal
Log in to your Click2Call account and navigate to Voice > Phones . Click Add New Phone and fill in the following details:
- Phone model: select your Yealink model from the drop-down menu
- MAC address: found on the sticker on the back of the phone
- Description: a label to identify this phone line (e.g. Reception, Sales)
- VLAN ID: optional — only required if your network uses VLANs
- Web admin password: set a password for the phone's web interface. If left blank, the default will be admin (admin) and user (user)
- Accounts: assign a phone number from the drop-down. If your Yealink model has multiple line keys, you can also configure speed dials, voicemail buttons, pickup, and transfer keys on the spare keys

Once complete, click Add New Phone to save.
[Image: Click2Call portal showing the Phone Manufacturer set to Yealink and the Phone Type drop-down listing all supported Yealink models]
### Find the phone's IP address
On the Yealink phone, press Menu and then select Status . The field labelled IPv4 Address shows the phone's current IP address on your network. Note this down — you will need it in the next step.
[Image: Yealink phone screen showing the main menu with Status selected (left) and the Status screen displaying the IPv4 Address (right)]
### Log in to the phone's web interface
Open a web browser on a computer connected to the same network and type the phone's IP address into the address bar (e.g. http://192.168.1.100 ). You will be redirected to the Yealink login page. Enter admin for both the username and password fields.
[Image: Yealink phone web interface login page showing username and password fields]
### Trigger Auto Provisioning
Once logged in, go to the Settings tab and select Auto Provision . In the Server URL field, enter:
http://yea.nz
Press Confirm at the bottom of the page. Once the page reloads, press Auto Provision Now . The phone screen will begin the auto-provisioning process. If a firmware upgrade is required, the phone will display a firmware update message.
[Image: Yealink phone web interface Auto Provision settings page with Server URL set to http://yea.nz]
### Wait for provisioning to complete
The provisioning process may take up to five minutes. The phone may reboot several times during this period — this is normal. Do not power off the phone during this process. Once finished, your Yealink handset will be ready to use. If you need to make any changes in future, update your settings in the Click2Call portal and reboot the phone to download the new configuration.
[Image: Yealink phone screen showing the firmware update and auto-provisioning process in progress]
Divider
Manual SIP Setup — All Brands
===== SECTION 2: Manual SIP Setup =====
## Option 2 — Manual SIP Setup
For Fanvil, Grandstream, Cisco, Polycom, and all other SIP-compatible phones
SIP Credentials Reference Table
## SIP Credentials Reference
Enter these values exactly as shown into your phone's SIP account settings.
| Username / Login / User ID | Your Click2Call phone number including country code e.g. 61234567890 |
|---||---|
| Authorisation Name / Display Name | Your Click2Call phone number including country code e.g. 61234567890 |
| Password | Your Click2Call password (assigned at signup) |
| Host / Proxy / Domain | sip.click2call.com.au |
| Outbound Proxy | sip.click2call.com.au |
| SIP Transport | UDP, TCP, or TLS — TLS preferred |
| SIP Port | 5060 or 50600 (UDP/TCP)  •  5061 (TLS) |
| DTMF Mode | RFC 2833 (also listed as AVT or Out-of-Band) |
| STUN Server | Not required |

Steps
### Connect the phone to your network
Plug the desk phone into your network using an Ethernet cable. If your switch supports PoE (Power over Ethernet), the phone will power on automatically. Otherwise, connect the included power adapter. Wait for the phone to fully boot before proceeding.
Screenshot placeholder
Screenshot placeholder — Step 1
### Access the phone's web interface
Find the phone's IP address — this is usually displayed on the phone screen under Menu > Status > Network . Open a web browser on a computer on the same network and navigate to that IP address (e.g. http://192.168.1.100 ). Log in with the phone's admin credentials (default is often admin / admin — check your phone's manual if this does not work).
Screenshot placeholder
Screenshot placeholder — Step 2
### Enter your SIP account settings
Navigate to the Account or SIP Account section in the phone's web interface. Enter the values from the SIP Credentials Reference table above. The field names vary by brand, but the mapping is consistent:
- Username / User ID / Account Name: your phone number including country code (e.g. 61234567890)
- Authorisation Name / Auth ID: same as username — your phone number including country code
- Password: your Click2Call password assigned at signup
- SIP Server / Host / Domain / Proxy: sip.click2call.com.au
- Outbound Proxy: sip.click2call.com.au
- SIP Port: 5060 or 50600 (UDP/TCP), or 5061 (TLS)
- DTMF Mode: RFC 2833 (also shown as AVT or Out-of-Band)
- STUN: leave blank or disabled — not required

Screenshot placeholder
Screenshot placeholder — Step 3
### Configure audio codecs
In the codec or audio settings section, enable the following codecs in order of preference. Disable any codecs not listed below to ensure the best call quality.
| Type | Supported Codecs |
|---||---|
| Voice (Audio) | G.711 alaw  •  G.711 ulaw  •  G.722  •  G.729a |
| Video | H.264  •  H.263 |

Important: Set the media packet time (ptime) to 20ms . A ptime of 10ms is not supported and will cause audio issues.
### Save settings and confirm registration
Save the account settings. The phone will reboot and attempt to register with the Click2Call network. A successful registration is confirmed by a green line key light , a Registered status on the phone screen, or a dial tone when you pick up the handset. This typically takes 30–60 seconds after saving.
Screenshot placeholder
Screenshot placeholder — Step 5
### Test a call
Dial another extension on your system or call your own number from a mobile to confirm the phone is working correctly. Check that audio is clear in both directions. If the phone fails to register, see the Firewall Rules section below and verify your credentials are entered exactly as shown.
Screenshot placeholder
Screenshot placeholder — Step 6
Firewall Rules
## Firewall Rules
If your phone sits behind a business firewall or router, allow the following traffic to ensure SIP registration and audio work correctly.
| Direction | Protocol | Port / Range | Purpose |
|---||---||---||---|
| Allow all traffic from | UDP | 1024 – 50000 | RTP media (voice audio) |
| Allow all traffic from | TCP | 5060 | SIP signalling (TCP) |
| Allow all traffic from | TCP | 5061 | SIP signalling (TLS) |

Apply these rules for traffic from subnet 52.63.154.234 . No STUN server is required.
Related articles
## Related Guides
Setting Up a Softphone App
Adding a New Extension
Poor Call Quality Troubleshooting
Still need help CTA
## Still need help?
Our Australian support team is available to assist you.
Contact Support
Call 1300 884 879
