## How to Use Reports &amp; Records | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-use-reports-and-records
**Description:** How to run call reports, search billing records, and export call data from the Reports and Records pages in the Click2Call portal.

===================== HEADER =====================
Breadcrumb
Article Header
Billing & Account
4 min read
# How to Use Reports & Records
The Click2Call portal provides two separate tools for reviewing your call activity: Reports and Records . Reports give you a summarised view of call activity grouped by line, making them useful for reviewing usage across your team. Records give you a detailed, line-by-line view of every individual call, which is useful for investigating specific calls, verifying charges, or exporting raw data for analysis.
Quick comparison box
### Reports vs Records — which one do you need?
|  | Reports | Records |
|---||---||---|
| What it shows | Summarised call totals per line | Individual call records |
| Best for | Usage overviews, team reporting | Investigating specific calls, charge verification |
| Export format | CSV | CSV |
| Email scheduling | Yes (daily, weekly, monthly) | No |

===== PART 1: REPORTS =====
## Part 1 — Reports
Account → Reports  • portal.click2call.com.au/account/reporting
Step 1
### Navigate to Account → Reports
In the left-hand sidebar, click Reports under the Account section. The page will load showing the Call Report by Line form. The breadcrumb at the top will show Account / Reports .
[Image: Reports page in the Click2Call portal showing the Call Report by Line form with Report type, Bill period, Calendar month, From/To Date, Record Types, Search Filter, and Email scheduling fields]
Step 2
### Configure the report filters
The Reports page contains several filters that control what data is included in your report. Here is a full explanation of every field.
| Field | Description |
|---||---|
| Report type | The type of report to generate. Call Report by Line is the default and shows call activity summarised per phone line or extension. |
| Bill period | The billing period to report on. Defaults to the current billing period. You can select a previous billing period from the dropdown to review historical data. |
| Calendar month | Filter results to a specific calendar month. Select All Months to include all months within the selected bill period. |
| From Date / To Date | Narrow the report to a specific date range within the selected bill period. Enter dates in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. 2026-03-01 ). Leave blank to include all dates in the period. |
| Record Types | Filter by the type of billing record to include. Options include All billing records , inbound calls, outbound calls, or specific call types. Use this to isolate particular types of activity. |
| Search Filter | A free-text search field. Enter a phone number, extension name, or partial string to filter results to matching lines only. |
| Email Call Reports by Line | Set a schedule to automatically email this report. Options include Never , daily, weekly, or monthly. When set, the report will be emailed to the address in the Report Email Recipient field on the selected schedule. |
| Report Email Recipient | The email address to send scheduled reports to. Click Update to save the email address after entering it. |

Step 3
### Generate or export the report
Once you have configured your filters, use one of the two action buttons:
- Submit — displays the report results on screen in the table below the form. The table shows each line with columns for Service, Name, Records, Answered, Busy, No Answer, Minutes, and Voice charges.
- Export — downloads the report as a CSV file without displaying it on screen. Use this when you want to open the data in a spreadsheet application such as Excel or Google Sheets.

Tip: The Totals row at the bottom of the on-screen report shows the sum of all lines. This is useful for quickly seeing your total call volume and minutes for the selected period.
===== PART 2: RECORDS =====
## Part 2 — Records
Account → Records  • portal.click2call.com.au/account/billingrecords
Step 4
### Navigate to Account → Records
In the left-hand sidebar, click Records under the Account section. The page will load showing the Billing Records search form. Unlike Reports, this page shows every individual call as a separate row, giving you a granular view of all activity on your account.
[Image: Billing Records page in the Click2Call portal showing search filters for Bill Period, Call Type, Calling Number, Called Number, From/To Date, Calls longer than, Billing Group, Answered Calls, Charged Items, and Results Limit]
Step 5
### Configure the search filters
The Billing Records page has a comprehensive set of filters to help you find exactly the calls you are looking for. Here is a full explanation of every field.
| Field | Description |
|---||---|
| Bill Period | The billing period to search within. Defaults to the current period. Select a previous period from the dropdown to search historical records. |
| Call Type | Filter by the type of call — for example, all call types, inbound, outbound, or specific call categories. Useful for isolating inbound-only or outbound-only activity. |
| Calling Number | The number that made the call (the originating number). Enter a full or partial number to search for calls from a specific caller. Format: 6492345678 . |
| Called Number | The number that was dialled (the destination number). Enter a full or partial number to search for calls to a specific number. Format: 64289212345 . |
| From Date / To Date | Narrow results to a specific date range. Enter dates in YYYY-MM-DD format. Leave blank to search across the entire selected bill period. |
| Calls longer than (seconds) | Filter out very short calls by entering a minimum duration in seconds. For example, entering 30 will only show calls that lasted 30 seconds or longer. Useful for excluding unanswered or very brief calls from your results. |
| Billing Group | If your account uses billing groups to separate lines or departments, select a specific group to filter results to that group only. Defaults to All Groups . |
| Answered Calls | When ticked, only calls that were answered will be included in the results. Untick to include all calls regardless of whether they were answered. |
| Charged Items | When ticked, only calls that incurred a charge will be included. This is useful for reviewing exactly which calls contributed to your bill. |
| Results Limit | The maximum number of records to return per search. Defaults to 10,000 . If your account has a very high call volume, you may need to narrow your date range to stay within this limit, or use the Export function to download the full dataset. |

Step 6
### Search or export billing records
Once you have set your filters, use one of the two action buttons:
- Search — displays matching call records on screen in a paginated table. Use the Results per page dropdown and the Previous / Next buttons to navigate through results.
- Export — downloads the matching records as a CSV file. The export respects all active filters, so you will only receive the records that match your search criteria.

Note: If the search returns No Results Found , check that the selected Bill Period contains activity and that your filters are not too restrictive. Try removing the date range or unchecking the Answered Calls and Charged Items filters to broaden the search.
FAQ Section
## Frequently asked questions
What is the difference between Reports and Records?
Reports provide a summarised view of call activity grouped by phone line or extension — showing totals for answered calls, busy calls, no-answer calls, total minutes, and charges. Records provide a detailed view of every individual call, including the exact calling and called numbers, call duration, date and time, and whether the call was charged. Use Reports for an overview and Records when you need to investigate specific calls.
Can I schedule reports to be emailed automatically?
Yes. On the Reports page, use the
Email Call Reports by Line
dropdown to select a schedule (daily, weekly, or monthly). Enter the recipient's email address in the
Report Email Recipient
field and click
Update
to save. The report will be emailed automatically on the selected schedule. This feature is available on the Reports page only — the Records page does not support scheduled email delivery.
How far back can I search call records?
Both Reports and Records are organised by billing period. You can select any previous billing period from the
Bill Period
dropdown to access historical data. Records are retained from the date your account was created, so you can search as far back as your account history allows.
My search returns No Results Found — what should I check?
First, confirm that the selected
Bill Period
is the correct one — it is easy to accidentally leave it on the current period when you intended to search a previous one. Next, remove any date range filters and uncheck the
Answered Calls
Charged Items
checkboxes to broaden the search. If you are searching by phone number, ensure the number is entered in the correct format (e.g.
6492345678
without spaces or dashes).
What does the Calls longer than field do?
This filter lets you exclude very short calls from your results. Enter a duration in seconds — for example, entering
will only show calls that lasted more than 10 seconds. This is useful for filtering out unanswered calls, voicemail drops, or very brief accidental calls that would otherwise clutter your results.
Can I export more than 10,000 records at once?
The default results limit is 10,000 records per search. If your account has more than 10,000 calls in a period, narrow the date range using the
From Date
To Date
fields and run multiple exports to cover the full period. For example, export the first two weeks of the month, then the second two weeks, and combine the files in a spreadsheet application.
What format are the dates in the exported CSV?
Dates in the exported CSV files are in
YYYY-MM-DD
format and times are in 24-hour format. If you are importing the CSV into accounting software or a reporting tool, you may need to reformat the date column to match the expected format of that application.
Can I see call recordings from the Records page?
The Billing Records page shows call metadata (date, time, numbers, duration, charges) but does not provide access to call recordings. Call recordings, if enabled on your account, are accessible from the
Media
section of the portal.
Related articles
### Related articles
Account History & Invoices Download PDF receipts and transaction history
How to Add Account Credit Top up your pre-pay balance
Setting Up a Call Flow Route inbound calls to the right destination
Back to Help Centre Browse all guides

## How to View Account History &amp; Download Invoices | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-view-account-history
**Description:** How to view your transaction history and download invoices and receipts from the Account History page in the Click2Call portal.

===================== HEADER =====================
Breadcrumb
Article Header
Billing & Account
2 min read
# How to View Account History & Download Invoices
The Account History page is where you can view every transaction on your Click2Call account and download PDF receipts or invoices for your records. This is useful for bookkeeping, expense claims, and providing proof of payment to your accountant or finance team.
Info box
### What you will find in Account History
- Every payment, top-up, and charge on your account listed in date order.
- A Reference number for each transaction.
- A PDF download icon to save a receipt or invoice for each transaction.
- An Export CSV option to download your full transaction history as a spreadsheet.

Steps
Step 1
## Log in to the portal
Go to portal.click2call.com.au and sign in with your account credentials. You will need to be the account administrator or have a user role with billing access to view transaction history.
Step 2
## Navigate to Account → History
In the left-hand sidebar, click History under the Account section. The breadcrumb at the top of the page will show Account / Account History confirming you are in the right place.
[Image: Account History page in the Click2Call portal showing transaction table with Date, Transaction Type, Amount, Reference/Download, and Export CSV columns]
Step 3
## Understanding the Account History table
The table lists every transaction on your account in reverse chronological order (most recent first). Here is what each column means.
| Column | Description |
|---||---|
| Date | The date the transaction was processed on your account. |
| Transaction Type | A description of what the transaction was — for example, a card payment, a manual top-up, a monthly line fee charge, or a credit adjustment. |
| Amount | The dollar value of the transaction in AUD. Negative values (shown as $ -x.xx) indicate a debit from your account balance. Positive values indicate a credit added to your account. |
| Reference / Download | The unique reference number for the transaction. Click the PDF icon (red) next to the reference to download a receipt as a PDF file. Click the paperclip icon to copy or view the direct link to the receipt. |
| Export CSV | Click this link on any row to download your complete transaction history as a CSV spreadsheet file, suitable for importing into accounting software such as Xero, MYOB, or QuickBooks. |

Step 4
## Download a receipt or invoice
To download a receipt for a specific transaction:
- Locate the transaction in the table using the Date and Transaction Type columns.
- In the Reference/Download column, click the red PDF icon on the right-hand side of the reference number.
- Your browser will download the receipt as a PDF file. The file name will include the transaction reference number for easy identification.

Tip: If you need to provide invoices to your accountant on a regular basis, use the Export CSV option to download all transactions at once rather than downloading individual PDFs.
Step 5
## Export your full transaction history as a CSV
The CSV export gives you a complete record of all transactions on your account in a single file. This is the fastest way to provide a full billing history to your finance team or accountant.
- On the Account History page, click the Export CSV link in the last column of any row.
- Your browser will download a .csv file containing all transactions.
- Open the file in Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, or your accounting software to view, filter, and sort the data.

Note: The CSV export includes all transactions — there is no date range filter. If you need to filter by date, open the file in a spreadsheet application and use the built-in filter tools.
FAQ Section
## Frequently asked questions
Can I download invoices for all my payments?
Yes. Every transaction that appears in the Account History table has a PDF download icon. Click the red PDF icon next to the reference number to download the receipt for that specific transaction.
Do the receipts include GST?
Yes. The PDF receipts are tax invoices and include a GST breakdown. They are issued by Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108) and are suitable for BAS reporting and expense claims.
What does a negative amount mean?
A negative amount (e.g. $ -5.00) means that amount was deducted from your account balance. This typically represents a payment made by your account to Click2Call — for example, a monthly line fee charge or a top-up payment processed from your saved card.
How far back does the Account History go?
The Account History page shows all transactions from the date your account was created. There is no limit on how far back the history goes. If you have a large number of transactions, use the Export CSV option to download the full list and sort or filter it in a spreadsheet application.
Can I filter transactions by date or type?
The Account History page does not have built-in date or type filters. To filter your transactions, export the history as a CSV file and open it in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets, where you can use column filters to view transactions by date range or transaction type.
I cannot see the PDF download icon — what should I do?
The PDF icon appears in the Reference/Download column to the right of the reference number. If you are on a smaller screen, you may need to scroll the table horizontally to see the icon. If the icon is still not visible, try refreshing the page. If the issue persists, contact support at
support@click2call.com.au
Can sub-users view the Account History?
Account History is visible to the account administrator. Sub-users with limited portal access may not see the History section in their sidebar. If a team member needs access to billing records, the account administrator can download and share the PDF receipts or CSV export directly.
A transaction is missing from my history — what should I do?
If you believe a transaction is missing, first try refreshing the page and scrolling to the bottom of the list. If it is still not visible, contact our support team at
support@click2call.com.au
or call
1300 884 879
and we will investigate.
Related articles
### Related articles
How to Add Account Credit Top up your pre-pay balance
Adding Additional Channels Increase simultaneous call capacity
Activating Your Account First steps after signing up
Back to Help Centre Browse all guides

## Viewing Call Recordings | Help Centre | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/help/how-to-view-call-recordings
**Description:** How to access, listen to, and download call recordings from the Click2Call portal.

===================== HEADER =====================
Article header
Breadcrumb
Call Flows & Routing
2 min read
# Viewing Call Recordings
If call recording is enabled on your account, all calls are automatically recorded and stored in the portal. This guide shows you how to find and listen to them.
Article body
Steps
Important: Call recording is disabled by default and must be manually enabled for each phone number or extension you wish to record. All parties must be aware that the call is being recorded to comply with Australian law.
## Part 1: How to Enable Call Recording
### Navigate to Call Recording Settings
Log in to the portal, go to the Voice tab, and select the line you want to configure. Click the Other Settings dropdown and choose Call Recording .
[Image: Navigate to Other Settings then Call Recording.]
### Configure and Enable Recording
In the Call Recording panel, untick the 'Disable ALL call recording' box. Choose your recording options, such as recording all calls or playing a message to the other party. You can also enter an email address to have a copy of all recordings sent to you automatically. Click Save Settings when done.
[Image: Call Recording configuration panel.]
## Part 2: How to View and Download Recordings
### Navigate to Billing Records
In the portal, click the Account tab, then select Records from the left-hand menu. This will take you to the Billing Records page where all your call history is stored.
[Image: Navigate to Account then Records.]
### Find, Play, and Download Your Recording
Use the filters to find the call you're looking for. On the far right of the call log entry, you will see a set of icons. These allow you to manage the recording for that specific call.
[Image: Call records list with recording icons highlighted.]
Here is what each icon does, from left to right:
[Image: Close-up of call recording icons.]
- Smiley Face: View AI-powered sentiment analysis for the call.
- Play/Stop: Listen to the call recording directly in your browser.
- List Icon: View the full AI-generated call transcription.
- Download: Download the call recording as an MP3 audio file.
- Trash Can: Permanently delete the recording.

Related articles
## Related Guides
Adding a New Extension
Setting Up the AI Receptionist
Contact Support
Still need help CTA
## Still need help?
Our Australian support team is available to assist you.
Contact Support
Call 1300 884 879

## Key Takeaways
- Click2Call is an Australian business communications provider trading as Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108), delivering cloud-based phone systems to businesses of all sizes across Australia.
- Core services include Cloud PBX, AI Voice Tools, AI Receptionist, SIP Trunks, and Microsoft Teams Direct Routing — all managed from a single online portal.
- No hardware or IT expertise is required. Businesses can be fully operational within one business day using existing internet connections and devices.
- Cloud PBX Landlines from $10/month (ex GST), Direct Dial Numbers from $5/month. No plans, no bundled minutes — pay only for what you use. No lock-in contracts and a 30-day free trial available.
- Click2Call is available to businesses across all of Australia , with Australian-hosted infrastructure and local support from an Australian-based team.

===================================================
       4. WHY CLICK2CALL
       ===================================================
## Why Do Australian Businesses Choose Click2Call?
We focus on what matters to a business owner: crystal-clear calls, zero setup headaches, and technology that actually earns its keep.
Click2Call is built and supported by a team with over 20 years of voice engineering experience — not a call centre.
Crystal Clear Voice
### Crystal Clear Voice
Click2Call uses HD voice codecs and Australian-hosted infrastructure to deliver call quality that is noticeably better than a traditional landline. Your calls are routed through data centres located in Australia, which means low latency and no international hops degrading your audio. Whether you are on a desk phone, laptop, or mobile app, every call sounds professional.
Instant Setup
### Instant Setup
There is no hardware to rack, no technician to book, and no waiting weeks for a line to be provisioned. You sign up online, configure your system through our intuitive web portal, and your team can start making and receiving calls within hours. For businesses migrating from an existing system, our team handles the number porting process end-to-end so there is zero downtime.
AI Powered
### AI Powered
Click2Call is not just a phone system — it is a communications platform with artificial intelligence built in. Our AI Receptionist answers calls around the clock, our Voice Tools transcribe and analyse every conversation, and our intelligent routing ensures callers always reach the right person. These are capabilities that previously required enterprise-level budgets, now available to any Australian business.
===================================================
       5. HOW IT WORKS
       ===================================================
## How Does Click2Call Work?
Getting started is straightforward. Most businesses are making calls within a single business day.
Connector line (desktop only)
Step 1
### Sign Up
Create your account at portal.click2call.com.au/join . Choose your plan, select how many seats you need, and pick your phone numbers. The whole process takes about five minutes and requires nothing more than a standard internet connection.
Step 2
### Configure
Use the Click2Call portal to set up your IVR menus, ring groups, business hours, voicemail greetings, and call routing rules. Everything is point-and-click — no technical knowledge required. If you need help, our Australian support team is available to assist with configuration at no extra charge.
Step 3
### Start Calling
Download the Click2Call softphone app on your computer or mobile, or plug in a compatible IP desk phone. Your team can immediately start making and receiving calls on their existing devices. If you are porting an existing number, calls will automatically switch over on the agreed porting date with no interruption to service.

       ===================================================
## Built for Australian Businesses Like Yours
Click2Call works across every industry. Here is how businesses like yours use it every day.
Real Estate
### Real Estate
Never miss an enquiry. Route calls to the right agent by suburb or listing, record every conversation for compliance, and let the AI Receptionist handle after-hours calls so leads are captured 24/7.
Tradies
### Tradies
Take calls on your mobile while on the tools. Use a local business number instead of your personal mobile, set up voicemail-to-email so you never lose a job lead, and add a second line when the business grows — no new hardware needed.
Medical Clinics
### Medical Clinics
Handle high call volumes without putting patients on hold. Use ring groups to distribute calls across reception staff, play custom on-hold messages with health information, and route after-hours calls to an answering service automatically.
IT Companies
### IT Companies
Integrate calling directly into Microsoft Teams or connect via SIP to your existing PBX. Use the API to build call workflows, trigger automations from inbound calls, and give remote staff a consistent business number wherever they work.

===================================================
       7. FAQ SECTION
       ===================================================
## Frequently Asked Questions About Click2Call
Straight answers to the questions business owners ask most.
What is Click2Call?
Click2Call is an Australian business communications provider, trading as Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108). We provide Cloud PBX phone systems, AI Voice Tools, AI Receptionist services, SIP Trunks, and Microsoft Teams Direct Routing to businesses of all sizes across Australia. Our platform replaces traditional on-premise phone hardware with a flexible, cloud-based system that is managed entirely online.
Is Click2Call available Australia-wide?
Yes. Click2Call serves businesses across all of Australia. Our services are delivered entirely over the internet, so there are no geographic restrictions. Whether you are in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, Hobart, Darwin, or a regional area, you can use Click2Call with any standard NBN or business internet connection.
How much does Click2Call cost?
Cloud PBX Landlines are $10.00/month (ex GST). Direct Dial Numbers are $5.00/month (ex GST). Microsoft Teams Lines are $10.00/month (ex GST). PBX Extensions are free. Every account includes 2 channels; additional channels are $3.00/month. Call rates from $0.10/min (AUS & NZ fixed). All prices ex GST. No lock-in contracts. See our full
pricing page
for details.
Do I need special hardware to use Click2Call?
No special hardware is required. You can use Click2Call with a standard internet connection and a computer, smartphone, or tablet using our free softphone apps. If you prefer physical desk phones, we can supply and pre-configure compatible IP phones that plug straight into your network — no additional hardware or cabling required.
Can I keep my existing business phone number?
Yes. We support number porting for most Australian geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08), 13/1300/1800 numbers, and mobile numbers. The porting process typically takes 5 to 10 business days and your service continues uninterrupted during the transfer. Our team manages the entire porting process on your behalf.
How long does setup take?
Most businesses are up and running within one business day. You sign up online, configure your system through our web portal, and start making and receiving calls. For more complex setups with custom IVR menus, integrations, or large numbers of users, our team can assist and typically completes configuration within 2 to 3 business days.
What internet speed do I need for VoIP?
Each simultaneous call requires approximately 100 Kbps of upload and download bandwidth. A standard NBN connection is more than sufficient for most businesses. For example, a 10-person office making 5 concurrent calls would need just 500 Kbps of dedicated bandwidth — a tiny fraction of a typical NBN 25 or NBN 50 plan.
Is there a lock-in contract?
No. Click2Call operates on a month-to-month basis with no lock-in contracts. You can scale up, scale down, or cancel at any time. We believe in earning your business every month through quality of service, not through contractual obligation. A 30-day free trial is available so you can test the platform before committing.
How is support provided?
Support is provided by our Australian-based team via phone (1300 884 879), email (support@click2call.com.au), and our online support portal. We offer business hours support as standard, with priority support available on higher-tier plans. When you call us, you speak to someone in Australia who understands the local telecommunications environment.
What makes Click2Call different from a traditional phone system?
A traditional phone system requires physical hardware installed on your premises, is expensive to maintain, and is difficult to scale. Click2Call is entirely cloud-based: there is no hardware to buy, no maintenance costs, and you can add or remove users in minutes from any web browser. You also get AI-powered features like automated receptionists, call transcription, and sentiment analysis that are simply not available on legacy systems — all at a fraction of the cost.
/faq-accordion
View all frequently asked questions →

## Microsoft Teams Calling Australia | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/microsoft-teams-calling
**Description:** Add real phone calls to Microsoft Teams with Click2Call

============================================================
  Click2Call — Shared Header Snippet
  File: _header.html
  Usage: Copy everything between the <header> tags (inclusive)
         into each page, immediately after <body>.
  Active state: Add class "c2c-nav-active" to the <a> or
                <button> that matches the current page.
  Tailwind CSS must be loaded in the page <head>:
  ============================================================
===================== HEADER =====================
=================== END HEADER ===================
── Header JavaScript ──
  Paste this <script> block just before </body> on each page,
  or consolidate into a shared site.js file.
SECTION 1: HERO
[Image: Business professional using Microsoft Teams for a phone call in a modern Australian office.]
# Make Real Phone Calls From Microsoft Teams
Your team already uses Microsoft Teams for chat and meetings. Click2Call adds one missing piece — the ability to call any phone number in Australia and receive calls on your business number, all from inside Teams. No new apps, no new hardware, no Microsoft Domestic Calling Plan required.
Get Started Free
View Pricing
SECTION 2: KEY TAKEAWAYS
Key Takeaways
- No new software: Your team keeps using Teams exactly as they do now. The dial pad just appears and they can call any phone number.
- What you need and what it costs: You need a Microsoft 365 Business plan (most Teams users already have this), a Microsoft Teams Phone System Licence ($12.10/month, paid to Microsoft), and a Click2Call Teams phone number ($10.00/month per line, paid to Click2Call). Call usage is charged at standard rates from a prepaid balance. Microsoft does not currently offer local Australian numbers or local calling in Australia — Click2Call provides both.
- Keep your existing number: Port your current Australian landline, 1300, or 1800 number to Click2Call. Your customers keep the same number they already know.
- Set it up yourself or we’ll do it: Add your number in the portal, enter your team’s email addresses, and run a script. Or pay a one-time $50/user fee and we handle everything for you.
- Australian hosted and supported: Our network is based in Australia. When you need help, you’re talking to our local team — not an overseas call centre.

SECTION 3: WHAT IS TEAMS CALLING?
## Teams Is Great — But It Can’t Call Phones Yet
Microsoft Teams is great for chatting with your team, sharing files, and jumping on a video call. But out of the box it can’t do one thing — call a regular phone number. You can’t ring a customer’s mobile, call a supplier, or receive a call from someone dialling your business number.
Click2Call fixes that. We connect your Teams account to the real phone network so your team can call and receive calls from any number in Australia — directly inside the Teams app they already use every day. Nothing changes for them except a dial pad appears.
SECTION 4: HOW IT WORKS
## How It Works
Three things connect together. That’s it.
### 1. Your Teams App
Your team keeps using Teams exactly as they do now. A dial pad appears and they can make and receive calls like a normal phone.
### 2. Click2Call Connects Them
We link your Teams account to our Australian phone network. This is the bridge that lets Teams talk to real phone numbers.
### 3. Any Phone Number in Australia
Once connected, your team can call any landline or mobile in Australia — and customers can call your business number and reach them in Teams.
This approach — called Direct Routing — is Microsoft-approved. It replaces Microsoft's Domestic Calling Plan (which isn't available at an affordable price in Australia) with a local provider like Click2Call.
SECTION 5: FEATURES GRID
## Everything You’d Expect From a Business Phone System
### Australian Numbers
Local numbers for any city, plus 1300 and 1800 numbers.
### No New Hardware
Works on any computer or phone your team already has.
### Stays Inside Teams
Your team never has to open another app.
### Call Recording
Record calls automatically and review them anytime.
### Voicemail to Your Inbox
Missed calls go to voicemail and land in your email.
### Keep Your Number
Transfer your existing number to Click2Call — one-time $50 porting fee.
### 1300 and 1800 Numbers
Point your 1300 or 1800 number straight to Teams.
### Australian Support Team
When something needs fixing, you’re talking to someone in Australia.
SECTION 6: COST COMPARISON
## What Does Microsoft Teams Calling Actually Cost?
There are three things you need to make calls from Teams. Two are paid to Microsoft, one is paid to Click2Call. Here is exactly what each one does and what it costs.
Prerequisite note
Before you start: You need a Microsoft 365 Business plan that includes Microsoft Teams (for example, Microsoft 365 Business Basic, Standard, or Premium). A personal Microsoft 365 subscription will not work — Teams calling requires a business plan. If your team already uses Teams for chat and meetings, you almost certainly have this already.
Cost breakdown cards
Card 1: Phone System Licence
Paid to Microsoft
### Microsoft Teams Phone System Licence
This is the licence that unlocks the dial pad inside Teams. Without it, Teams has no phone functionality at all — you can chat and video call your colleagues, but you cannot call a real phone number. It does not include an Australian number or local calling — that is what Click2Call provides.
$12.10
/user/month
Included in Microsoft 365 E5, or available as an add-on to any Microsoft 365 Business plan. Paid directly to Microsoft.
Card 2: Click2Call Teams Line
Paid to Click2Call
### Click2Call Teams Phone Number
This gives you an Australian phone number and connects it to Teams via Direct Routing. Microsoft does not offer local Australian numbers or affordable local calling in Australia — Click2Call provides both. One number is needed per Teams user who needs to make and receive calls.
$10.00
/number/month (ex GST)
Paid to Click2Call. Includes your Australian number and line rental.
Card 3: Call Usage
Paid to Click2Call
### Call Usage
Calls are charged at our standard rates from a prepaid credit balance. You top up your account and calls are deducted as you make them. There are no lock-in contracts and no minimum monthly call spend.
Pay as you go
Standard Australian call rates. View our full rate card on the Pricing page.
Summary comparison table
| Cost Component (per user/month) | Paid to | Amount |
|---||---||---|
| Microsoft 365 Business Plan (must include Teams — Business Basic, Standard, or Premium) | Microsoft | Varies by plan |
| Microsoft Teams Phone System Licence (required to enable the dial pad in Teams) | Microsoft | $12.10/month |
| Click2Call Teams Phone Number (Australian number + local call termination via Direct Routing) | Click2Call | $10.00/month (ex GST) |
| Call Usage (local, mobile, and international calls) | Click2Call | Standard rates (prepaid balance) |
| Estimated Fixed Monthly Cost (per user, excl. call usage & M365 plan) |  | From $22.10/month (ex GST) |

Microsoft pricing sourced from microsoft.com, February 2026. Subject to change. The Microsoft Teams Phone System Licence is paid directly to Microsoft — Click2Call does not charge for this. Click2Call charges $10.00/month per number for the Teams phone line, plus call usage at standard rates from a prepaid balance. Microsoft does not currently offer local Australian numbers or local calling in Australia.
SECTION 7: SETUP PROCESS
## Setting It Up Yourself Takes About 30 Minutes
### Add Your Teams Number
In the Click2Call portal go to Account → Numbers, add a new number and select “Microsoft Teams” as the line type. One number is needed per Teams user.
[Image: Click2Call portal showing how to add a Microsoft Teams phone number by selecting Microsoft Teams as the line type]
### Assign Your Team Members
In Voice → Teams, enter each person’s Microsoft 365 email address next to their phone number. The portal generates the PowerShell commands for you automatically.
[Image: Click2Call portal showing the Microsoft Teams Lines screen where each team member is assigned a phone number]
### Run the Script and You’re Done
Copy and run the generated PowerShell commands in Microsoft Teams Admin. The dial pad appears in Teams within a few minutes.
[Image: Click2Call portal showing the auto-generated Microsoft Teams PowerShell commands ready to copy and run — no manual scripting required]
SECTION 7B: DIY VS MANAGED SETUP
## Set It Up Yourself, or Let Us Do It
The setup involves running some PowerShell commands in Microsoft Teams Admin. It’s not hard, but if you’d rather hand it over, we’ve got you.
DIY Card
### Do It Yourself
- Add a Teams number in the portal (Account → Numbers)
- Assign each person’s Teams email address in Voice → Teams
- Copy and run the PowerShell commands the portal generates for you automatically
- Takes about 30 minutes if you have Teams Admin access

Free no setup fee
See the Steps Above
Managed Setup Card
We Handle Everything
### Managed Setup
- You provide your team’s Microsoft 365 email addresses
- We configure everything in the portal and run the Microsoft setup commands
- Up and running within 1–2 business days
- Includes testing to confirm every user can call and receive

$50.00 per user (ex GST) — one-time fee
Get Started — We’ll Handle the Rest
Both options use the same Click2Call portal and Australian phone network. The managed setup is a one-time fee — no ongoing managed service charges.
SECTION 8: FAQ
## Common Questions
What is Microsoft Teams Direct Routing?
Direct Routing is the way Click2Call connects your Microsoft Teams to real phone numbers. It’s a Microsoft-approved method that lets your team call any landline or mobile in Australia — and receive calls on your business number — all from inside Teams.
Do I need a Microsoft Phone System licence for Direct Routing?
Yes. Each Teams user needs a Microsoft 365 licence that includes the Phone System add-on. Click2Call provides the phone number and the connection to the phone network. Microsoft provides the software side. Contact us if you’re not sure which licence you have — we can check.
How does Direct Routing differ from Microsoft Calling Plans?
Microsoft Calling Plans are Microsoft's own calling service, which can be expensive and are not always available in all Australian regions. Direct Routing uses a third-party provider like Click2Call to supply the calling capability, which is typically 40–60% cheaper than Microsoft Calling Plans and offers more flexibility, including Australian local numbers and 1300 numbers.
Can I keep my existing phone numbers with Click2Call Teams Calling?
Yes. Click2Call supports full number porting, so you can transfer your existing Australian landline or 1300 numbers to our platform and use them with Microsoft Teams. The porting process typically takes 5–10 business days and is managed by our team.
What equipment do I need for Microsoft Teams Calling?
No additional hardware is required. Your team can make and receive calls directly from the Microsoft Teams desktop app, mobile app, or a Teams-certified desk phone. If you prefer physical handsets, we can supply compatible Teams-certified IP phones.
Is Click2Call's Teams Calling available across all of Australia?
Yes. Click2Call's Microsoft Teams Direct Routing service is available to any Australian business with a stable internet connection, regardless of location. We provide Australian local numbers for all major cities and regions, as well as 1300 and 1800 numbers.
How long does it take to set up Teams Direct Routing?
If you set it up yourself using our portal, it usually takes about 30 minutes once you have your number. If you choose our managed setup service ($50 per user, one-time), we handle everything and have you running within 1–2 business days.
What internet connection speed do I need for Teams Calling?
Microsoft Teams requires approximately 100 kbps per concurrent call. A standard NBN connection is more than sufficient for most businesses. We recommend a connection with low jitter and latency for the best call quality. Our team can advise on network requirements during the setup process.
Does Click2Call offer a free trial for Teams Calling?
Yes. Click2Call offers a 30-day free trial for all services, including Microsoft Teams Direct Routing. You can sign up at portal.click2call.com.au/join and our team will assist with the initial configuration.
What happens if Microsoft Teams has an outage?
In the event of a Microsoft Teams service disruption, Click2Call can configure call failover to redirect incoming calls to a mobile number, alternative number, or voicemail. This ensures your business remains reachable even during platform outages.
Have more questions? View all FAQs for answers to common questions about our services.
SECTION 9: CTA
## Add Calling to Teams Today
Sign up free, add a Teams number in the portal, and have your team making calls in Teams by end of day.
Get Started

SECTION 6 — AUSTRALIAN CALL RATES
## Australian Call Rates
All rates exclude GST. Standard calls are charged in 1-minute increments.
| Call Type | Flagfall A flagfall is a one-time connection fee charged at the start of each call, in addition to any per-minute rate. | Per Minute |
|---||---||---|
| Fixed (local & national) | — | $0.10 |
| Mobile | — | $0.20 |
| Calls TO 13/1300 numbers | $0.50 | Free * |
| 1300 inbound (to your 1300) | — | $0.20 |
| 1800 inbound (to your 1800) | — | $0.20 |
| Emergency (000) | — | Free |
| Inbound calls answered on PC app | — | Free |

* Calls TO 13 and 1300 numbers are charged a flat $0.50 connection fee per call. There is no additional per-minute charge regardless of call duration.
† For 1300 numbers, your caller pays their local call rate. For 1800 numbers, the call is free for your caller — you pay the $0.20/min inbound rate.
Note:
Inbound calls answered on the Click2Call PC app are always free. Routing inbound calls to a mobile phone number incurs your standard $0.20/min rate.

SECTION 10 — PRICING FAQ
## Pricing Questions
Is there a minimum spend or monthly fee?
No. Click2Call has no monthly plan fees or minimum spend requirements. You only pay for your phone number ($10.00/month ex GST) and the calls you make. If you make no calls in a month, you only pay the number fee.
What happens when my credit runs out?
When your credit balance reaches zero, outbound calls will be suspended until you top up. Inbound calls answered on the PC app will continue to work. We recommend setting up automatic top-up in the customer portal to ensure uninterrupted service.
How do I load credit?
Log in to the customer portal at portal.click2call.com.au and go to the Payments section. We accept all major credit cards and direct debit. We recommend loading a minimum of $50.00 to start.
Can I set a low-balance alert?
Yes. You can configure low-balance notifications in the customer portal so you receive an email alert when your credit drops below a threshold you set. This helps ensure you never unexpectedly run out of credit.
Are inbound calls free?
Inbound calls answered on the Click2Call PC app are always free. If you route inbound calls to a mobile phone number, your standard rate of $0.20/min applies. If you have a 1300 or 1800 number, inbound calls to that number are charged at $0.20/min to your account regardless of how they are answered.
Is there a contract or lock-in period?
No. Click2Call is completely month-to-month with no lock-in contracts. You can cancel your account at any time via the customer portal. Unused credit is refundable — contact us at click2call@bcomservices.com.
Can I port my existing phone number?
Yes. We can port most Australian landline and mobile numbers. A one-time porting fee of $50.00 (ex GST) applies. Porting typically takes 5–10 business days and your number remains active throughout the process — there is no downtime.
What is a call channel?
A call channel allows one simultaneous call — either inbound or outbound. Your account includes 2 channels free. If you need more than 2 calls at the same time, add extra channels for $3.00/month each (ex GST). As a guide, a 5-person office typically needs 2–3 channels.
How much does it cost to call a 1300 number?
Calls to 13 and 1300 numbers are charged a flat $0.50 connection fee per call. There is no additional per-minute charge regardless of how long the call lasts.
What is the difference between a 1300 and 1800 number?
With a 1300 number, your caller pays their local call rate to reach you. With an 1800 number, the call is completely free for your caller — you pay the $0.20/min inbound rate. Both number types cost $10.00/month (ex GST) on Click2Call.
Do you charge GST?
Yes. Click2Call is an Australian business (ABN: 92 636 893 108). GST of 10% applies to all charges. All prices on this page are shown excluding GST. Your tax invoice will show the ex-GST amount and GST separately.
How do I get started?
Fill in our signup form at portal.click2call.com.au/join — it takes about 5 minutes. Account activation takes up to 24 hours. Once active, load your first credit and you are ready to make calls.
SECTION 11 — BOTTOM CTA

Login to Portal
### New Customers

Get Started Free

Hero
[Image: Abstract background image]
# Privacy Policy
Key Takeaways
## Key Takeaways
- Click2Call is operated by Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108).
- We collect only the personal information necessary to provide our telecommunications services.
- We do not sell your personal data to third parties.
- You may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal information at any time.
- Contact us at support@click2call.com.au for any privacy enquiries.

Our commitment to protecting your personal information in accordance with Australian privacy law.
Privacy Policy Content
### Legal Review Recommended
This privacy policy is a template and does not constitute legal advice. We strongly recommend you have it reviewed by a qualified legal professional to ensure it meets all specific requirements for your business and jurisdiction.
This Privacy Policy outlines how Bcom Services Pty Ltd (ABN: 92 636 893 108) , trading as Click2Call ("we", "us", or "our"), collects, uses, discloses, and manages personal information in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) ("Privacy Act") and the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs).
## 1. What personal information we collect
We may collect and hold personal information about you, that is, information that can identify you, such as your name, address, email address, phone number, job title, company name, and payment or billing information (including credit card details).
## 2. How we collect personal information
We collect personal information directly from you when you:
- Enquire about our services.
- Sign up for a free trial or purchase a service.
- Contact our support team.
- Use our website, services, or customer portal.
- Respond to a survey or marketing communication.

We may also collect information from third parties, such as from a publicly available source or a third party that provides marketing leads.
## 3. Why we collect and how we use personal information
We collect, hold, use, and disclose your personal information for the following purposes:
- To provide you with our products and services.
- To manage and administer our services, including billing and collection.
- To communicate with you about your account and provide customer support.
- To improve our products and services.
- To conduct marketing and promotional activities, from which you can opt-out at any time.
- To comply with our legal and regulatory obligations.

## 4. Disclosure to third parties
We may disclose your personal information to third parties in connection with the purposes described above. This may include:
- Our upstream telecommunications carriers and service providers.
- Our technology partners, including data centres and software providers.
- Payment processors and financial institutions.
- Our professional advisers, such as lawyers and accountants.
- Government and regulatory authorities as required by law.

## 5. Overseas disclosure
Some of the third parties to whom we disclose personal information may be located overseas. We take reasonable steps to ensure that any overseas recipient of your personal information does not breach the APPs in relation to your personal information.
## 6. Security of personal information
We take reasonable steps to protect your personal information from misuse, interference, loss, and from unauthorised access, modification, or disclosure. We use a range of physical, administrative, and technical measures to protect your personal information.
## 7. Access and correction
You have a right to access the personal information we hold about you and to request that it be corrected. To request access or correction, please contact us using the details below. We may require you to verify your identity before providing access.
## 8. Complaints
If you have a complaint about our handling of your personal information, please contact us using the details below. We will investigate your complaint and attempt to resolve it. If you are not satisfied with our response, you can contact the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC).
## 9. Contact Us
For any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or our privacy practices, please contact us at:
Email: support@click2call.com.au Phone: 1300 884 879
This policy was last updated on 23 February 2026.

## SIP Trunks Australia | Business SIP Trunk Provider | Click2Call

**URL:** https://www.click2call.com.au/sip-trunks
**Description:** Connect your PBX to our network with quality SIP Trunks. Get flexible, scalable, and cost-effective voice connectivity for your Australian business.

============================================================
  Click2Call — Shared Header Snippet
  File: _header.html

── Header JavaScript ──
  Paste this <script> block just before </body> on each page,
  or consolidate into a shared site.js file.
SECTION 1: HERO
[Image: Abstract image of data flowing through a network server rack, representing SIP trunk connectivity.]
# Business SIP Trunks for Australia
Connect your existing on-premise or cloud PBX to our Tier-1 Australian voice network. Click2Call SIP Trunks deliver superior call quality, redundancy, and scalability, replacing your outdated ISDN lines with a flexible, cost-effective VoIP solution. Keep your PBX, keep your numbers, and upgrade your connectivity.
View SIP Trunk Pricing
SECTION 2: KEY TAKEAWAYS
Key Takeaways
- ISDN Replacement: SIP trunks are the modern, NBN-ready technology to replace traditional ISDN and PSTN phone lines.
- Keep Your PBX: Our SIP trunks are compatible with most major IP PBX systems, including 3CX, FreePBX, Asterisk, Cisco, and Avaya.
- Cost Savings: Significantly reduce your monthly line rental costs compared to ISDN and benefit from competitive call rates.
- Scalability & Flexibility: Easily add or remove call channels as your business needs change, without needing new physical lines installed.
- Australian Network: All calls are routed through our redundant, high-availability network hosted entirely within Australia for optimal quality and reliability.

SECTION 3: WHAT IS A SIP TRUNK?
## What Is a SIP Trunk?
A SIP Trunk is the modern, digital equivalent of a traditional telephone line. Instead of a physical copper wire running to your building, a SIP trunk uses your business internet connection to link your phone system (PBX) to the public telephone network. 'SIP' stands for Session Initiation Protocol, which is the open-standard technology that establishes and manages voice calls over the internet (VoIP).
Replacing ISDN and Analogue Lines
For decades, businesses relied on ISDN (Integrated Services Digital Network) or older analogue PSTN lines to make and receive calls. These technologies are now obsolete and being phased out across Australia as part of the NBN rollout. A SIP trunk is the direct replacement. It provides the same functionality—making and receiving multiple calls at once—but does so more efficiently and cost-effectively over your data connection. Each 'channel' in a SIP trunk allows for one concurrent call, so a 10-channel SIP trunk is the equivalent of 10 traditional phone lines.
SECTION 4: WHY SWITCH?
## Why Switch to SIP Trunks?
### Cost Savings
Eliminate expensive ISDN line rentals and enjoy lower call rates.
### Flexibility
Scale your call capacity up or down in minutes, not weeks.
### Redundancy
Route calls to backup numbers or locations instantly if your PBX goes offline.
### Geographic Freedom
Use any local number from any Australian city, regardless of your physical location.
SECTION 5: FEATURES
## Carrier-Grade Features Included
### Australian Numbers
Bring your numbers or get new ones from any area code.
### Redundant Carriers
Automatic failover across multiple Tier-1 voice carriers.
### Number Porting
Keep all your existing business phone numbers.
### 1300/1800 Numbers
Terminate your national inbound numbers to your PBX.
### Per-Second Billing
Only pay for what you use with fair, per-second billing on calls.
### No Hardware Required
Connects directly to your existing IP-enabled PBX.
### Works with your PBX
Certified for use with 3CX, FreePBX, Asterisk and more.
### Local Aussie Support
Get help from our expert team based in Australia.
SECTION 6: COMPARISON TABLE
## SIP Trunks vs Traditional Phone Lines
| Feature | Traditional Lines (ISDN) | Click2Call SIP Trunks |
|---||---||---|
| Technology | Physical copper lines | Virtual lines over internet (VoIP) |
| Scalability | Slow, requires new line installation | Instant, add channels on demand |
| Cost | High monthly rental per line | Low monthly cost per channel |
| Disaster Recovery | Difficult, tied to physical location | Easy, instantly re-route numbers |
| NBN Readiness | Not compatible, being phased out | NBN-native technology |

SECTION 7: COMPATIBLE PBX
## Works With Your Existing PBX
Our SIP trunks are built on open standards, ensuring compatibility with any SIP-enabled IP PBX.
FreePBX
Asterisk
Yealink
Grandstream
SECTION 8: FAQ
## SIP Trunk FAQs
What is a SIP trunk?
A SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunk is a virtual phone line that connects your business phone system (PBX) to the Public Switched Telephone Network (PSTN) via the internet. Instead of traditional copper phone lines, SIP trunks use your existing internet connection to carry voice calls, enabling significant cost savings and greater flexibility.
How many SIP channels do I need?
Each SIP channel supports one simultaneous call. As a general rule, you need roughly one channel for every 3–4 staff members who make calls concurrently. For example, a business with 20 staff might need 5–8 channels. Click2Call can help you assess your call volume and recommend the right number of channels.
Can I keep my existing phone numbers when switching to SIP trunks?
Yes. Click2Call supports full number porting for Australian geographic numbers (02, 03, 07, 08 prefixes), 1300 numbers, and 1800 numbers. Our team manages the entire porting process, which typically takes 5–10 business days.
What PBX systems are compatible with Click2Call SIP trunks?
Click2Call SIP trunks are compatible with all major IP PBX systems, including 3CX, FreePBX, Asterisk, Cisco, Avaya, Grandstream, and Yealink. If you are unsure whether your existing PBX is compatible, contact our team and we will confirm compatibility before you sign up.
Is there a minimum contract term for SIP trunks?
No. Click2Call SIP trunks are available on a month-to-month basis with no lock-in contracts. You can add or remove channels at any time through the customer portal, and you only pay for what you use.
What internet connection do I need for SIP trunks?
A standard NBN connection is sufficient for most businesses. Each SIP channel requires approximately 100 kbps of bandwidth. We recommend a connection with low latency and jitter for optimal call quality. A dedicated internet connection or QoS (Quality of Service) configuration is recommended for businesses with high call volumes.
Are SIP trunks available Australia-wide?
Yes. Click2Call SIP trunks are available to any Australian business with an internet connection. We provide Australian geographic numbers for all states and territories, as well as 1300 and 1800 numbers.
How quickly can I get SIP trunks set up?
New SIP trunk services can typically be provisioned within 24 hours for new numbers. If you are porting existing numbers, the process takes 5–10 business days. Our team will guide you through the setup and configuration.
What is the difference between SIP trunks and a Cloud PBX?
A SIP trunk connects your existing on-premise or hosted PBX to the telephone network. A Cloud PBX replaces your entire phone system with a fully hosted solution in the cloud. If you already have a PBX you are happy with, SIP trunks are the most cost-effective way to modernise your calling. If you want a complete, managed phone system, a Cloud PBX is the better choice.
Does Click2Call offer a free trial for SIP trunks?
Yes. Click2Call offers a 30-day free trial for all services, including SIP trunks. You can sign up at portal.click2call.com.au/join and our team will assist with the initial configuration and testing.
Have more questions? View all FAQs for answers to common questions about our services.
SECTION 9: CTA
## Upgrade Your Voice Connectivity
Move to a more flexible, reliable and cost-effective voice solution. Talk to our team about a custom quote.
Contact Sales

# Click2Call Support Centre
Our Australian-based team is here to help. Find answers in our help categories below, or get in touch directly.
## Key Takeaways
- Click2Call provides Australian-based technical support via phone, email, and customer portal.
- Call 1300 884 879 for urgent technical issues or billing enquiries.
- Log in to the customer portal at portal.click2call.com.au to manage your account and raise support tickets.
- Most common issues can be resolved quickly using the troubleshooting guides on this page.
- Click2Call's infrastructure is hosted in Australian data centres, designed for reliable, always-on business communications.

### Call or Email Us

Common Troubleshooting
## Common Troubleshooting Topics
### My calls have poor audio quality
Poor call quality is almost always caused by the internet connection, not our platform. Check that your connection is stable, that you have sufficient bandwidth (at least 100kbps per call), and that no other applications are consuming large amounts of bandwidth during calls. Enabling QoS (Quality of Service) on your router to prioritise VoIP traffic is highly recommended.
### I cannot make or receive calls
First, check your internet connection is working. Then verify your device (phone or softphone) is registered to our platform — you should see a green "Registered" status in your device settings. If you are using a desk phone, try restarting it. If the issue persists, please contact our support team.
### I am not receiving voicemails by email
Check that your voicemail-to-email address is correctly configured in the portal. Also check your spam/junk folder, as voicemail notification emails can sometimes be filtered. If the email address is correct and emails are not arriving, please contact support.
### My auto-attendant is not routing calls correctly
Log in to the customer portal and review your call flow configuration. Ensure that the correct extensions or ring groups are assigned to each menu option. If you need help setting up a complex call flow, our support team can assist you.
### How do I add a new user to my account?
New users can be added directly through the customer portal. Navigate to the Users section, click "Add User", and follow the prompts. The new user will receive a welcome email with their login credentials and setup instructions.

# Terms and Conditions
The legal agreement governing your use of Click2Call's VoIP and Cloud PBX services.
Terms and Conditions Content
Last Updated: 9 March 2026  •  Effective Date: 9 March 2026  •  Jurisdiction: Australia (Queensland)
These Terms and Conditions govern the use of telecommunications services provided under the Click2Call brand operated by BCOM Services Pty Ltd (ABN 92 636 893 108) .
---
## 1. Agreement and Acceptance
These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") form a legally binding agreement between Click2Call, a service operated by BCOM Services Pty Ltd ("Click2Call", "we", "us", "our"), and the customer ("you", "client", "customer").
By creating an account, activating services, loading account credit, or using Click2Call services, you agree to be bound by these Terms.
These Terms operate under applicable Australian laws including the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Australian Consumer Law), the Telecommunications Act 1997 , the Privacy Act 1988 , and the Fair Trading Act 1989 (QLD).
---
## 2. Service Provider
Click2Call is a cloud communications and telecommunications platform operated by:
BCOM Services Pty Ltd ABN: 92 636 893 108 Queensland, Australia
Click2Call provides hosted telecommunications services including Cloud PBX systems, VoIP phone services, SIP trunking, and related communications services.
---
## 3. Description of Services
Click2Call provides cloud-based telecommunications services including:
- Hosted Cloud PBX systems
- VoIP business phone systems
- SIP trunking services
- Direct Inward Dial numbers (DIDs)
- Microsoft Teams Phone integration
- Call routing and call management services
- Telecommunications consulting and system configuration

Services rely on internet connectivity and third-party carrier infrastructure. All telecommunications services operate in accordance with the Telecommunications Act 1997 and applicable regulatory obligations administered by the Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA).
---
## 4. Account Registration
To use Click2Call services, customers must create an account and provide accurate information including name or business name, contact details, and billing information. Customers are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of their account credentials. Click2Call may suspend accounts where inaccurate or fraudulent information is provided.
---
## 5. Pricing and Charges
All pricing is in Australian Dollars (AUD) and is shown exclusive of GST (ex GST) . GST of 10% will be added at the time of payment when you top up your account credit. Charges may include:
- Monthly service fees
- Phone number rental
- Call usage charges
- Hardware or device purchases
- Professional services or setup fees

Call charges may vary depending on call destination and telecommunications carrier routing.
---
## 6. Prepaid Billing Model
Click2Call operates on a prepaid billing system. Customers must load funds into their account before services can be used. Services are deducted from the customer's account balance including call usage, monthly service fees, phone number rental, and additional service features. Customers are responsible for maintaining sufficient account credit. Where account credit is insufficient, Click2Call may restrict or suspend services until additional credit is added.
---
## 7. Account Credit and Refund Policy
Funds added to a Click2Call account are treated as prepaid service credit. Unless required by Australian law:
- Account credit is non-refundable
- Unused credit remains available while the account remains active

Click2Call may refuse refund requests except where required under Australian Consumer Law.
---
## 8. Service Cancellation
Click2Call services operate without lock-in contracts. Customers may cancel services at any time. Upon cancellation:
- Active services will cease
- Remaining prepaid credit will remain on the account but is not refundable
- Telephone numbers may be lost unless ported to another provider before cancellation

Customers are responsible for initiating number porting if they wish to retain their telephone numbers.
---
## 9. Fair Use Policy – Unlimited Calling
Certain plans may include unlimited outbound calling to Australian and New Zealand numbers. This is intended for normal business communication.
Included calls: Australian landlines, Australian mobile numbers, New Zealand landlines, and New Zealand mobile numbers.
Excluded usage includes: telemarketing campaigns, call centre operations, automated dialling systems, bulk outbound calling, premium rate numbers, and international destinations outside AU/NZ.
Click2Call may apply additional charges where usage significantly exceeds normal business patterns.
---
## 10. Telecommunications Infrastructure
Click2Call services depend on third-party infrastructure including internet service providers, NBN networks, telecommunications carriers, and data centre infrastructure. Click2Call does not control these networks and cannot guarantee uninterrupted service. Service disruptions may occur due to carrier outages, internet provider faults, or infrastructure failures.
---
## 11. VoIP Service Quality and Internet Dependency
VoIP services rely on the customer's internet connection and network environment. Call quality may be affected by internet speed and stability, network congestion, Wi-Fi quality, router or firewall configuration, and packet loss, latency, or jitter. Click2Call does not guarantee call quality where issues originate from the customer's network or internet service provider. Customers are responsible for ensuring their network is suitable for VoIP communications.
---
## 12. Emergency Services (000)
VoIP services depend on internet connectivity and electrical power. During power outages or internet failures, access to 000 emergency services may not be available. Customers should maintain an alternative communication method such as a mobile phone for emergency use. Click2Call does not guarantee emergency calling availability during service outages.
---
## 13. Telephone Numbers and Porting
Telephone numbers supplied through Click2Call may be provisioned through third-party carriers. Click2Call does not guarantee permanent ownership of numbers supplied through hosted services. Number porting requests are subject to carrier approval, accurate account information, and accounts being paid in full. If services are cancelled without porting the number, the number may be returned to the carrier.
---
## 14. Acceptable Use Policy
Click2Call services must only be used for lawful telecommunications purposes. Prohibited uses include:
- Scam calls or fraudulent activity
- Robocalling or mass automated dialling
- Telemarketing without lawful consent
- Sending spam communications
- Caller ID spoofing intended to deceive
- Harassment or abusive communications

Click2Call may suspend or terminate services where misuse is detected.
---
## 15. Fraud Prevention and Toll Fraud
Customers are responsible for securing their account credentials and telecommunications equipment. Click2Call is not responsible for fraudulent use of an account including compromised SIP credentials, unsecured PBX systems, or hacked devices or endpoints. All call charges generated through a customer account remain the responsibility of the account holder. Click2Call may implement fraud monitoring but cannot guarantee prevention of toll fraud.
---
## 16. Service Suspension for Security Risk
Click2Call may suspend or restrict services where activity suggests account compromise, telecommunications fraud, unusual call patterns, or breach of these Terms. Suspension may occur without prior notice to protect the customer and telecommunications network.
---
## 17. Call Recording and Privacy
Click2Call systems may support call recording features. Customers are responsible for ensuring call recording complies with applicable laws including the Privacy Act 1988 , Australian Privacy Principles, and the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (QLD). Customers must obtain appropriate consent where required. Click2Call is not responsible for how customers store or use recorded communications.
---
## 18. Limitation of Liability
To the maximum extent permitted by law, Click2Call is not liable for loss of business, loss of revenue or profits, data loss, indirect or consequential damages, or service interruptions caused by third-party infrastructure. Nothing in these Terms excludes rights under Australian Consumer Law.
---
## 19. Privacy
Click2Call complies with the Privacy Act 1988 and the Australian Privacy Principles. Customer information is used only for service delivery, billing and administration, and legal compliance. Full details are available in the Click2Call Privacy Policy .
---
## 22. Service and Support Availability
Click2Call provides customer service and technical support during standard business hours, Monday to Friday 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST), excluding public holidays.
Click2Call will make reasonable efforts to respond to support requests in a timely manner. While Click2Call aims to respond to support enquiries within four (4) hours, response times are not guaranteed.
Limited after-hours support may be available outside standard business hours. After-hours support is provided on a best-effort basis only and may not be available for all service requests.
Click2Call does not guarantee response times, fault resolution, or service restoration outside standard business hours.
Customers acknowledge that Click2Call does not provide a 24/7 support service unless a separate support agreement has been entered into.
---
## 23. Service Availability vs Support Availability
Click2Call telecommunications services are designed to operate 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. However, service availability does not imply continuous support availability.
While the Click2Call platform and telecommunications infrastructure may operate continuously, technical support services are provided during standard business hours only, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
Customers acknowledge that service interruptions or faults occurring outside business hours may not be investigated until the next business day.
Customers requiring guaranteed response times, priority incident management, or 24/7 support coverage must enter into a separate written Service Level Agreement (SLA).
---
## 24. Network Maintenance and Planned Service Interruptions
Click2Call may perform scheduled maintenance, upgrades, or infrastructure changes necessary to maintain the reliability, security, and performance of the telecommunications platform. Maintenance activities may include:
- Software updates
- Network upgrades
- Security patches
- Carrier infrastructure changes
- System improvements

Where reasonably possible, maintenance will be scheduled during low usage periods. Customers acknowledge that temporary service interruptions may occur during planned maintenance activities. Click2Call is not liable for service interruptions caused by planned maintenance or system upgrades.
Emergency maintenance required to address security risks or service instability may be performed without prior notice.
---
## 25. Regulatory Compliance and Lawful Interception
Click2Call operates in accordance with Australian telecommunications legislation and regulatory obligations. Click2Call may be required to comply with lawful requests or directions issued under applicable laws including the Telecommunications Act 1997 (Cth) and other relevant legislation. This may include obligations to:
- Provide assistance to authorised government agencies
- Enable lawful interception or access to telecommunications data where required by law
- Provide subscriber information where legally requested by authorised authorities

Click2Call will only disclose customer information where required or permitted under Australian law. Customers acknowledge that Click2Call must comply with such legal obligations and may be required to provide information or access without prior notice where legally mandated.
---
## 26. Governing Law
These Terms are governed by the laws of Queensland and the Commonwealth of Australia.
---
## 27. Contact Information
Click2Call A service operated by BCOM Services Pty Ltd ABN: 92 636 893 108
Phone: 1300 884 879 Email: support@click2call.com.au Website: www.click2call.com.au

## Key Takeaways
- No technician, no hardware. You set up your entire Adelaide phone system online in under an hour — no site visits, no waiting.
